title: [FEATURE]: Remember users last model choice (VSCode)
body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

Currently, for any new session in the extension we default to Kilo: Auto. While this should be the default for any new user, we should remember a users' last selected model and use that instead going for new sessions.

For existing sessions, those should remain unaffected.
author: lambertjosh

comments.author: markijbema
comments.body: I am not sure we want this, this kind of making a local choice global was very confusing in the old model, and made it almost impossible to run more than one session. I'm very much against reintroducing this global state.

comments.author: Githubguy132010
comments.body: I do agree with @lambertjosh It's quite annoying to change the model every time. And besides, not every task needs Opus or Sonnet,

comments.author: lambertjosh
comments.body:
> I am not sure we want this, this kind of making a local choice global was very confusing in the old model, and made it almost impossible to run more than one session. I'm very much against reintroducing this global state.

@markijbema - can you elaborate more on the problem with running more than one session? I was thinking this would just affect new session starts and any session already existing would continue to use the model that it was using. Not sure if that helps address your concern or not.

comments.author: markijbema
comments.body: It wouldnt, i only switch models for a specific reason, for instance, typically i write with opus, and then sometimes i like to use GPT because it isnt adding as much comments, but then I wouldnt' expect all my new sessions in all my vscodes to start with gpt as well. This is actually one of my biggest frustrations with the legacy extension

comments.author: markijbema
comments.body: And @lambertjosh 's suggestion  does make sense if you conceptually only have one session (sidebar in vscode, one repo; maybe restart task, but then continue where you were), but if you have a lot you end up using the wrong model a lot. I fear that it will make it easier for starting users but scare away advanced users, and we don't have a proper alternative for them (like a standalone agent manager)

comments.author: lambertjosh
comments.body:
> And @lambertjosh 's suggestion does make sense if you conceptually only have one session (sidebar in vscode, one repo; maybe restart task, but then continue where you were), but if you have a lot you end up using the wrong model a lot. 

Ah, I tend to use multiple windows/CLI's for separate sessions. Do you use Worktrees in a single VSCode to keep different changesets isolated? Curious about your workflow here.

Maybe a solution here is to put the default model setting option very front and center on the settings area? I would it annoying to have to go in and dig around for it anytime I wanted to change it. I flip around a few times per day for various reasons, so reasonably often.

comments.author: markijbema
comments.body: in the old extension multiple windows does not isolate, and the you didn't propose that isolation above either. I mostly use multiple vscodes because that was the best workflow with the legacy extension, but I'm trying out working in agent manager more

comments.author: lambertjosh
comments.body:
> in the old extension multiple windows does not isolate, and the you didn't propose that isolation above either. I mostly use multiple vscodes because that was the best workflow with the legacy extension, but I'm trying out working in agent manager more

I'm not sure isolation really has an impact on this setting, I was more just curious about your workflow. TBH I'm not sure isolation within a single VSCode window is that helpful unless you are also using them across different worktrees... but that's kind of the job for Agent Manager.

comments.author: lambertjosh
comments.body:
Circling back to the design, would this setting impact Agent Manager? 

I think we want to avoid someone having an out of date default model, not knowing they can change and/or manage it. This space moves quickly with new versions of models like Opus 4.5/4.6, as well as other model launches. The free model space moves even more rapidly. We could try to solve this by:
1. Remembering the last used model (you have concerns on this)
1. Offering some method to persist the model you have selected as the default, without having to dip into settings.

I'm kind of wary of adding yet more buttons and options, though. Do you have other ideas @markijbema ?

comments.author: markijbema
comments.body:
I have no problem with 'remembering' as such, but if we change it for all common sessions we also 'spread' it so to say, thus making it impossible to do a temporary different model choice. Also, when we fix that model choice is tethered to mode again this will be even more confusing, as people will sometimes see the model they chose, and sometimes not (depending on their mode). I think all the implicit ways to change the default model was one of the major confusions in the legacy extension.

My major gripe is that if i change anything in the prompt box it is a local change, except for (in this proposal) the model change, because that would change all sessions going forward (unless it's a different mode). I think it should be clear to the user that they're doing this, and not an implicit action.

I don't think that going to the settings once every week or so should be a problem, if it is I wonder why the settings are so scary and/or why is it so complex to get there (and if it is, people would probably be better of sticking to kilo/auto)

In the end I think it's weighing usecases against eachother and considering how bad it is to have a little friction for the user to get what they want, version to have less friction, but the user not getting what they want because something unexpected happened. I think the second problem is a way worse problem to have

comments.author: markijbema
comments.body:
Just checked, in cursor changing the model once does change it, but they *don't* have a model tied to a mode, which is something our users like very much and we promote a lot as well. I think the combination of the two is especially confusing as you don't actually change the model going forward (because a mode change will revert to some other model), and you don't change it for just now. I think we need something consistent there.

I think Cursors solution works (but they are less powerful than us in mode use), and what I suggest works (only ever changing in the settings + i think we need to show the model for all modes, not just the 'default model'). I just think the middle ground is especially confusing

comments.author: markijbema
comments.body:
Maybe we should make the design more explicit than 'remember last choice'.

I currently have model Opus everywhere:
```
A. a plan session on local in agentmanager
B. a code session in the sidebar
C. a debug session on worktree X in agentmanager
D. an ask session on worktree X in agentmanager
E. a code session on worktree  Y in agent manager
F. a code session on a different repo in another vscode
```

I change my model in B from Opus to GPT. 

now in the next hour i start:

```
G. a plan session on local in agentmanager
H. a code session in the sidebar
I. a debug session on worktree X in agentmanager
J. an ask session on worktree X in agentmanager
K. a code session on worktree  Y in agent manager
L. a code session on a different repo in another vscode
```

which session would we expect to be Opus, and which session would we expect to be GPT?

comments.author: iscekic
comments.body: I wanted to pick this one up, but it's non-actionable at the moment. Fwiw in the old extension we explicitly wanted to remember model per mode. Changing a model changes it for that session, new sessions fallback to the default.

comments.author: lambertjosh
comments.body:
We should definitely at least make this better. The current setting of just using Kilo Auto on every single start is very annoying, I find, as it happens mid-session.

I also agree we definitely want to keep the power and flexibility of per-mode model defaults, which does complicate the settings a little bit. (opencode calls them agents, we should just use what the CLI calls them for now)

How about the following for now:

1. Model defaults do not apply mid-session. If you override a model, exit the extension, and come back your model should not be changed underneath you.
2. Model defaults are _per-mode_ if set. This aligns with opencode: https://opencode.ai/docs/agents#mode
3. If a default model for a mode no longer exists, it reverts back to the default. Note that we will need to maintain any mode model defaults as models rotate and change.
4. 3. If you change a model in a session, it does not change the default. (We can explore popping an option to do this in context, but that can come later)

Thoughts?

comments.author: markijbema
comments.body: Seems good to me.

comments.author: iscekic
comments.body:
I'd like to propose something (in my mind) simpler:
- we always remember the last model you selected - makes the extension/CLI very simple, no surprises
- model per mode stuff is controlled via settings, persisted on the backend, we introduce a new virtual model `kilo/custom` - this way cloud features can reuse it too

I think it nicely unifies cloud <-> local (imo settings should live in our backend anyway so they can be synced around - imagine a world where you log into cli and can immediately start working because all your settings were synced).

It also completes the virtual model list:
- auto - we pick
- auto-free - we pick, but free
- small - we pick, but small
- custom - users pick

comments.author: lambertjosh
comments.body:
>    model per mode stuff is controlled via settings, persisted on the backend, we introduce a new virtual model kilo/custom - this way cloud features can reuse it too
> 
> I think it nicely unifies cloud <-> local (imo settings should live in our backend anyway so they can be synced around - imagine a world where you log into cli and can immediately start working because all your settings were synced).

To make sure I follow @iscekic - so we'd only "show" 4 models, and you would be able to control which of the 4 you used... as well as which model was behind Kilo Custom? These would be sync'd to the cloud, etc. Or is Kilo Custom in addition to the other larger set of models we'd offer?

comments.author: iscekic
comments.body:
> we'd only "show" 4 models

We'd show both virtual and non-virtual models, as we do today.

It's just another virtual model, synced to the cloud. It allows you to pick a model per mode.

comments.author: markijbema
comments.body: @iscekic a lot of people would like to have provider choices for those as well though

comments.author: iscekic
comments.body:
Decision:

Add a new section in the provider settings screen - model per mode - table with 3 columns:
mode -> provider -> model.

On boot, we read the settings and use whatever the user has set. Falls back to default above.

When a user changes a model for a mode, the override lasts until the extension reboots.

Additional bug to fix: after setting a default, cannot unset (use server default).

Every task submission causes immediate extension host termination with exit code 132 (SIGILL). The crash is 100% reproducible.

Crash signature:
[Extension Host] [createTask] parent task ... instantiated
Extension host (LocalProcess pid: XXXXXX) terminated unexpectedly. Code: 132, Signal: unknown

Impact: 
Complete loss of functionality on AMD Ryzen + Ubuntu 24.04.

Only Kilo Code

All versions >5.6.0

[Extension Host] [createTask] parent task ... instantiated
Extension host (LocalProcess pid: XXXXXX) terminated unexpectedly. Code: 132, Signal: unknown

Ubuntu 24.04 with AMD Ryzen hardware

GNOME terminal, VS Code launched from GUI application menu.

title: Windows terminal inserts escape sequences after CLI exit when moving mouse
body:
## Bug
After closing the Kilo CLI on Windows, moving the mouse in the terminal inserts raw escape sequences like `[555;27;21M`.

These are SGR mouse tracking events that should have been disabled on exit.

## Expected Behavior
Mouse tracking should be properly disabled when the CLI exits, regardless of exit path (normal exit, Ctrl+C, error).

## Environment
- OS: Windows
- Terminal: Any (PowerShell, CMD, etc.)

## Proposed Solution
Add explicit terminal state reset sequences for all mouse tracking modes (1000, 1002, 1003, 1006, 1015) plus Kitty keyboard protocol on all exit paths.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

1. Description

Requesting native integration for Alibaba Bailian (DashScope) in Kilo. The Tongyi Qwen series—especially the latest qwen3.5-plus—has shown exceptional performance in coding and logical reasoning. As a top-tier choice for developers, native support would significantly optimize the user experience.

While it is currently possible to connect via the "Custom OpenAI" interface, it is cumbersome to manually configure the Base URL and type in complex Model IDs every time.

Pre-configure the API endpoint and provide a dropdown menu for common models to eliminate manual entry.

Official Qwen Models: qwen3.5-plus (Core Flagship), qwen3-max-2026-01-23, qwen3-coder-next, qwen3-coder-plus

Hosted Third-party Models: glm-4.7, kimi-k2.5

4. Technical Information

Base URL: https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1

Protocol: OpenAI API Compatible

Reference: [Alibaba Cloud Model Studio Coding Plan](https://help.aliyun.com/zh/model-studio/coding-plan?mode=pure#dc0d98da6ev4j)

5. Why This Feature is Needed

Powerful Performance: qwen3.5-plus and the Coder series have reached or even exceeded Claude 3.5 Sonnet's level in many code generation benchmarks.

Ultra-Low Latency: Compared to overseas models, Bailian offers extremely fast access speeds within the region. This provides a noticeable boost to the fluidity of Autocomplete features.

Ecosystem Integration: Bailian serves as a hub for multiple top-tier models (Qwen, GLM, Kimi, etc.). Supporting this single platform grants users access to an entire ecosystem of leading LLMs.

[x] I have verified that this feature request has not been suggested before.

[x] I am willing to participate in beta testing once the feature is available.

title: VS Code Extension: Add Custom OpenAI-Compatible Provider UI
body:
## Problem

The VS Code extension currently lacks a UI for adding custom OpenAI-compatible providers, even though this functionality exists in the web/TUI application (`packages/app`).

Users must manually edit their `opencode.json` configuration file to add OpenAI-compatible providers, which creates a poor user experience and friction for users who want to:
- Connect to local models (Ollama, LM Studio)
- Use custom API endpoints
- Connect to enterprise/self-hosted deployments
- Use lesser-known OpenAI-compatible providers

## Current State

### Web/TUI App (`packages/app`) ✅
Has full UI support for custom providers:
- `dialog-custom-provider.tsx` - Full form dialog with validation
- `settings-providers.tsx` - Settings section with "Custom provider" button (line 212-234)

Features:
- Provider ID input with validation
- Provider name input
- Base URL input with format validation
- API Key input (supports both direct keys and `{env:VAR}` format)
- Multiple model entries (ID + name)
- Custom headers support
- Full form validation
- Integration with global config updates

### VS Code Extension (`packages/kilo-vscode`) ❌
Missing the custom provider UI:
- `ProvidersTab.tsx` only shows:
  - Default model selector
  - Small model selector
  - Disabled providers list
  - Enabled providers list
- No UI to add/configure custom providers

## Current Workaround

Users must manually create/edit `opencode.json`:

```json
{
  "provider": {
    "my-custom-provider": {
      "name": "My Custom Provider",
      "api": "https://api.example.com/v1",
      "npm": "@ai-sdk/openai-compatible",
      "options": {
        "apiKey": "key-here",
        "baseURL": "https://api.example.com/v1"
      },
      "models": {
        "model-id": {
          "id": "model-id",
          "name": "Model Name",
          "temperature": true,
          "tool_call": true,
          "cost": {
            "input": 0.0001,
            "output": 0.0002
          },
          "limit": {
            "context": 128000,
            "output": 4096
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Proposed Solution

Port the custom provider UI from `packages/app` to `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui`:

1. **Add `DialogCustomProvider` component** - Port from `packages/app/src/components/dialog-custom-provider.tsx`
2. **Update `ProvidersTab`** - Add a "Custom Provider" section similar to the web app
3. **Add necessary translations** - Ensure all i18n keys exist in the VSCode webview translations
4. **Add backend integration** - Use existing `handleUpdateConfig` and auth endpoints

## Implementation Notes

- The VSCode extension already has the necessary backend plumbing via `KiloProvider.handleUpdateConfig()`
- The backend `/global/config` endpoint supports updating provider configurations
- Auth can be set via the `/global/auth/set` endpoint
- The UI should follow the same validation logic as the web app

## Related Documentation

- [OpenAI Compatible Providers docs](https://kilo.ai/docs/ai-providers/openai-compatible)
- Provider system implementation in `packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts`
- Config schema in `packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts`

## User Impact

High - This is a frequently requested feature that significantly improves the user experience for developers using:
- Local LLM setups
- Custom API gateways
- Enterprise deployments
- Self-hosted solutions

title: [CLI] Toggling MCP servers in CLI/TUI does not persist to config file
body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before. Recreating [from](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/issues/6292)

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

### Description

Toggling MCP servers via CLI/TUI (connect/disconnect) only modifies runtime state and does not persist to the config file. Changes are lost after application restart.

## Root Cause History
### June 29, 2025 - PR Kilo-Org/kilo#513 (commit f0962e2d)
- Added enabled field to MCP config schema
- Purpose: Allow users to disable MCP servers at startup by setting enabled: false in config
- Implementation: The create() function checks if (mcp.enabled === false) and skips connecting to those servers
- This was designed for manual config file editing only
### December 8, 2025 - PR #4509 (commit 203f3312e)
- Added runtime toggle functionality via TUI dialog and server endpoints (/mcp/{name}/connect, /mcp/{name}/disconnect)
- Missed integration: The new connect()/disconnect() functions never persist to the enabled field introduced in June
- Result: Toggle works in memory but doesn't update the config, so changes don't survive restarts
## The Gap
The December 2025 feature should have bridged these two systems:
- June's enabled field (static config)
- December's toggle endpoints (runtime control)
Instead, they remain disconnected - June's feature still requires manual config editing, while December's feature only affects the current session.

### Plugins

none

### Kilo version

1.0.24

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Start CLI/TUI
2. Open MCP dialog (TUI: Ctrl+X, select "Toggle MCPs")
3. Toggle an MCP server (press Space)
4. Restart CLI/TUI
5. Server state reverts

### Screenshot and/or share link

_No response_

### Operating System

macOS 26.3

### Terminal

Terminal inside of VSCode

title: [BUG] kilo-docs: ZenMux provider doc is inconsistent with other provider docs and missing from sidebar nav
body:
### Description

#### Bug Description

The `pages/ai-providers/zenmux.md` doc has several issues:

1. **Not in sidebar navigation** — the page exists but is not listed in `lib/nav/ai-providers.ts`, so users cannot find it from the sidebar
2. **Inconsistent format** — missing `sidebar_label` frontmatter and `**Website:**` link that all other provider docs have
3. **Contains unrelated content** — large blocks of API code examples (OpenAI SDK, Anthropic SDK, Google Vertex AI, Platform API) that belong to ZenMux's own docs, not the Kilo Code integration guide
4. **Redundant sections** — product feature descriptions (Automatic Routing, Fallback, Cost Optimization, ZDR) and Advanced Configuration that duplicate each other

#### Expected Behavior

The ZenMux doc should follow the same format as other provider docs (e.g., `openrouter.md`):

- Listed in sidebar navigation
- Standard sections: Getting an API Key, Configuration in Kilo Code, Tips and Notes
- Only Kilo Code-relevant settings documented

#### Affected Files

- `packages/kilo-docs/pages/ai-providers/zenmux.md`
- `packages/kilo-docs/lib/nav/ai-providers.ts`

title: [FEATURE]: Allow tools to stop the agent loop via metadata
body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

## Summary

By default, invoking a tool will trigger another assistant turn.

In some cases it's useful if the tool can terminate the agent loop, e.g. for task-complete signals, early exits or any other tool that should end the session without a follow-up model response.

(An example would be a speak tool, that provides a summary via TTS, which must stop the loop to prevent the agent responding to itself)

## Feature proposal

1) Allow tools to return a structured output `{ title?, output, metadata? }` in addition to a plain string, to be able to set metadata
2) A metadata flag that halts the loop

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

### Description

When working in a CLI environment, having keybinds for regularly used functions is extremely useful.

A few keybinds for toggles (found in ctrl+p menu) are missing:

`console_toggle` - to toggle console
`timestamps_toggle` - to toggle timestamps display
`diffwrap_toggle` - to toggle diff wrap

Additionally, it would be very useful to have keybinds that could toggle certain MCP slots directly
author: pschiel

title: feat: Learn Mode agent for AI-mentored coding
body:
## Summary

Add a **Learn Mode** native agent that acts as a Socratic coding mentor. It implements code fully, then pauses to ask 2-3 targeted comprehension questions to reinforce understanding — turning passive code generation into active learning.

## Motivation

AI coding assistants risk turning developers into passive consumers of generated code. Learn Mode addresses this by:

- Asking targeted questions after non-trivial implementations (referencing specific identifiers, file paths, line numbers)
- Tracking comprehension over time with per-session state and cross-session project-scoped aggregates
- Auto-calibrating difficulty based on recent answer quality (beginner → intermediate → advanced)
- Providing session learning logs with understood concepts, gaps, and suggested next steps

## Implementation

| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| Learn Agent prompt | Socratic mentor system prompt with question rules, difficulty calibration, escape hatches |
| Learn Tracker | Session + aggregate persistence, auto-calibration from last 5 answers |
| `learnwrite` / `learnread` tools | Record comprehension checks and read learning state |
| Server routes | `GET /session/:id/learn` + `GET /session/learn-aggregate` |
| TUI indicator | Live level + check count display when in learn mode |
| Tests | ~900 lines of test coverage for tracker and tools |

## Context

This feature won the Kilo Code hackathon. PR: #6118

title: Bug: CONTRIBUTING.md missing issue template requirements causing agent-created issues to be auto-closed
body:
## Description

CONTRIBUTING.md does not document the required fields for GitHub issue templates, causing issues created via the `gh` CLI or API (which bypass the web UI template) to be automatically closed by the compliance bot within 2 hours.

The compliance bot references CONTRIBUTING.md as the 'contributing guidelines', but that document does not explain what is required. Additionally, the bot links to `CONTRIBUTING.md#pr-titles` and `CONTRIBUTING.md#issue-first-policy` anchors that do not exist in the file.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Use `gh issue create` to create a bug report or feature request
2. The web UI template is bypassed
3. The AI compliance bot flags the issue as non-compliant
4. The issue is auto-closed after 2 hours
5. The bot message says to see CONTRIBUTING.md, but that doc has no relevant guidance

> Originally tracked as Kilo-Org/kilocode#6290

title: [FEATURE]: add kilo help --all command and auto-generated CLI reference docs
body:
- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

## Describe the enhancement you want to request

Add a `kilo help --all` command that outputs the complete CLI reference — all commands and subcommands — as Markdown or plain text. Also auto-generate CLI reference documentation from the help output, eliminating manual maintenance of the command table in `cli.md`.

### Motivation

- Useful for generating LLM context files, documentation, and quick offline reference
- The hand-written command table in `cli.md` is already stale (lists `kilo github` which is disabled, missing `acp` and `help`)
- Every command add/remove/rename requires a manual docs update that is easy to forget

### Proposed API

```
kilo help --all              # full reference, markdown (default)
kilo help --all --format text  # plain text
kilo help auth               # scoped to a single command + its subcommands
```

> Originally tracked as Kilo-Org/kilocode#6294 and Kilo-Org/kilocode#6291
author: maphew

title: LSP server processes are not killed properly in Windows
body:
### Description

On Windows, after LSP usage (e.g. biome) and exiting Kilo CLI, the LSP server processes don't get killed and survive as zombies.

This will cause Kilo to hang on the next startup, unless the entire terminal is restarted or the processes killed manually.

### Problem

`ChildProcess.kill()` sends `SIGTERM`, which Windows processes ignore (see https://nodejs.org/api/child_process.html#subprocesskillsignal).

Additionally, biome lsp-proxy spawns its own child processes, so even if the parent was killed, the children remained as zombies.
author: pschiel

title: Windows path issues
body:
### Description

A whole series of bugs, resulting from filepath issues with backslashes occuring on Windows.

(copied from https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/issues/6332)

## Observed issues

- `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` returns `E:/x/y` forward slash format → worktree/directory mismatch
- `path.resolve()`, `path.relative()`, `realpathSync.native()`, `realpath` (bash) break with git bash paths (various issues)
- e.g.: `/d/x` within C drive (`/c/`) results in things like `C:\d\x`
- relative paths not working like expected using cross-drive
- `contains` logic not working → issues with permission system and external_directory
- `/tmp` inside git bash is something else outside of it
- tools break using backslash paths (some backslashes get "eaten")
- escaping hell for agents → requires double/quadruple escaping
- observed in all win32 native shells (git bash, cmd, powershell/pwsh)
- few issues in app/desktop (wrong filepath splits/usage)
- bun hard crashing with segfault when `spawn()` is used with non-existing directory

## How to reproduce/test

- run test suite (unit/feature) in windows (currently they run only with linux in the pipeline)
- running prompts with various problematic/mixed filepaths

comments.author: shssoichiro
comments.body:
In my opinion it would be a useful feature to pull forward, though I have seen the dissenting material from Anthropic as to why Claude Code does not have indexing.

### Pros
- Improved search capability, particularly for large codebases or when the exact function name is not known
- Reduced number of tool calls compared to grep

### Cons
- Requires managing indexing state to ensure it is up-to-date with codebase (handled well by legacy Kilo Code)
- Indexing can be time consuming depending on codebase size and embedding provider
- Quality of results depends on quality of embeddings

title: [FEATURE]: Agent-scoped MCP tool filtering
body:
## Problem

When using MCP multiplexers (e.g., a single MCP server that aggregates tools from many backends), all agents currently see the full set of MCP tools. This creates problems for specialized agent topologies:

- **Token overhead**: A multiplexer can expose 200+ tools; agents that only need 5-10 pay the full context cost on every turn.
- **Model confusion**: More tools = more opportunities for the model to pick the wrong one, especially for focused subagents (reviewer, explorer).
- **No specialization**: You can't give an "ops" subagent access to Google Workspace + calendar tools while keeping a "code" subagent lean with just codebase search.

## Proposed Solution

Add an optional `mcpToolPrefixes` property to the agent config. When set, only MCP tools whose key starts with one of the listed prefixes (boundary-safe) are included in that agent's tool list.

### Example config

```json
{
  "agent": {
    "code": {
      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
      "mcpToolPrefixes": ["governor_codebase_rag", "governor_context7"]
    },
    "reviewer": {
      "mode": "subagent",
      "model": "openai/o3",
      "mcpToolPrefixes": ["governor_codebase_rag", "governor_supabase"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Design constraints

- Fully backward compatible (optional, no default)
- Boundary-safe matching: prefix `foo` matches `foo_bar` but NOT `foobar`
- Prefix sanitization consistent with MCP tool key construction
- Empty strings rejected at schema level

## Prior art

This was previously submitted as PR #554 on `Kilo-Org/kilo` before the repo migration. kiloconnect bot reviewed and recommended merge. Re-submitting here per maintainer request.

Migrated from: https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilo/issues/555

title: Doesn't work with local models
body:
### Description

title: [FEATURE]: Allow tools to stop the agent loop via metadata
body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

## Summary

By default, invoking a tool will trigger another assistant turn.

In some cases it's useful if the tool can terminate the agent loop, e.g. for task-complete signals, early exits or any other tool that should end the session without a follow-up model response.

(An example would be a speak tool, that provides a summary via TTS, which must stop the loop to prevent the agent responding to itself)

## Feature proposal

1) Allow tools to return a structured output `{ title?, output, metadata? }` in addition to a plain string, to be able to set metadata
2) A metadata flag that halts the loop

title: Bug: CONTRIBUTING.md missing issue template requirements causing agent-created issues to be auto-closed
body:
## Description
CONTRIBUTING.md does not document the required fields for GitHub issue templates, causing issues created via the `gh` CLI or API (which bypass the web UI template) to be automatically closed by the compliance bot within 2 hours.

The compliance bot references CONTRIBUTING.md as the 'contributing guidelines', but that document does not explain what is required. Additionally, the bot links to `CONTRIBUTING.md#pr-titles` and `CONTRIBUTING.md#issue-first-policy` anchors that do not exist in the file.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Use `gh issue create` to create a bug report or feature request
2. The web UI template is bypassed
3. The AI compliance bot flags the issue as non-compliant
4. The issue is auto-closed after 2 hours
5. The bot message says to see CONTRIBUTING.md, but that doc has no relevant guidance

## Impact
- Agents (AI coding assistants) creating issues on behalf of users will have their issues auto-closed
- Human contributors using the CLI will have their issues auto-closed
- The referenced CONTRIBUTING.md provides no actionable guidance to fix the problem

## Expected Behavior
CONTRIBUTING.md should document all required template fields, the compliance enforcement rules, and provide exact `gh` CLI commands for creating compliant issues.
author: app/kilo-code-bot

comments.author: Olusammytee
comments.body: Implemented in Kilo-Org/kilo#596.\n\nThis updates CONTRIBUTING.md with required issue-template fields for CLI/API-created issues, adds the missing #issue-first-policy and #pr-titles anchors, and includes compliant gh examples.

title: Snapshot Git Objects Growing to 68GB - Needs Automatic git gc
body:
# Issue: Snapshot Git Objects Growing to 68GB - Needs Automatic git gc

## Description

The kilo snapshot storage in `~/.local/share/kilo/snapshot/global/objects/` grew to **68GB** on my system, filling 95% of my 233GB disk. This appears to be caused by git object accumulation without automatic garbage collection.

## Environment
- OS: Linux
- Kilo version: Latest
- Disk: 233GB (95% full before fix)

## Current Behavior

The snapshot directory structure appears to be a git repository:
```
~/.local/share/kilo/snapshot/global/
├── objects/     <- This grew to 68GB
├── refs/
├── hooks/
└── ...
```

## Solution That Worked

Running git garbage collection dramatically reduced the size:

```bash
cd ~/.local/share/kilo/snapshot/global
git gc --aggressive --prune=now
```

**Result:** 68GB → 288MB (99.6% reduction!)

## Related Issues
- #536 - Kilocode takes up a lot of disk space (closed, but focused on task history)
- Discussion #5406 - tasks checkpoints in project as a single repository

## Proposed Solutions

### Option 1: Automatic git gc
Run `git gc --aggressive --prune=now` periodically or when:
- Snapshot size exceeds a threshold (e.g., 5GB)
- On VSCode startup if last gc was > 7 days ago
- Before creating new checkpoints

### Option 2: Git configuration
Set git config to auto-gc more aggressively:
```bash
git config gc.auto 256
git config gc.autoDetach false
```

### Option 3: User-facing feature
Add a "Cleanup Snapshots" button in Settings > Checkpoints similar to the existing task cleanup feature.

## Temporary Workaround for Users

Add this to a weekly cron:
```bash
0 2 * * 0 cd ~/.local/share/kilo/snapshot/global && git gc --aggressive --prune=now
```

Or create a script to monitor and auto-clean:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR="$HOME/.local/share/kilo/snapshot/global"
SIZE_GB=$(du -sg "$SNAPSHOT_DIR" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1)
if [ "$SIZE_GB" -gt 10 ]; then
    cd "$SNAPSHOT_DIR" && git gc --aggressive --prune=now
fi
```

## Impact

This issue can cause:
- Disk space exhaustion
- System instability
- VSCode slowdowns
- User frustration when trying to save files

The fix is simple and safe - git gc is a standard git maintenance operation that compresses loose objects without losing data.

---

Would the maintainers be open to a PR that adds automatic snapshot garbage collection?
author: mehrdadbn9

comments.author: peterkc
comments.body:
The snapshot system already has garbage collection and runs on startup and hourly via the scheduler. The 68GB growth you experienced suggests the existing gc parameters aren't aggressive enough for heavy workloads. Here's what the code reveals about why:

## Existing Snapshot Maintenance

| Commit | What it added |
|--------|--------------|
| [`e2f1f4d`](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/commit/e2f1f4d81e) | Scheduler + `git gc --prune=7.days` running hourly |
| [`ac0b37a`](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/commit/ac0b37a7b7) | `info/exclude` syncing to respect project-level excludes |

`Snapshot.cleanup()` in `snapshot/index.ts` runs `git gc --prune=7.days` on the snapshot repository. It's registered with the scheduler on startup (runs immediately, then hourly). The snapshot directory lives at `~/.local/share/kilo/snapshot/<project-id>/` — in your case, `global` is the project ID used when git can't derive one from root commits.

## Why 68GB Still Accumulates

**1. 7-day prune window vs. high creation rate:** `Snapshot.track()` is called at both `start-step` and `finish-step` in the session processor. Each call runs `git add .` then `write-tree`, creating loose tree and blob objects. With `--prune=7.days`, all objects from the past week survive gc regardless. For an active user generating hundreds of snapshots per day, a week of accumulated loose objects can be massive.

**2. `write-tree` without commits:** The snapshot system creates tree objects but never creates commits or refs pointing to them. These remain as loose unreferenced objects until the prune window expires. Without commit chains, git's pack optimization (delta compression between similar objects) is less effective during standard gc.

**3. The bulk of your fix was `--prune=now`:** Your manual `gc --aggressive --prune=now` achieved 99.6% reduction (68GB to 288MB). The primary contributor to that reduction is likely `--prune=now` eliminating the entire 7-day backlog of loose objects, rather than the `--aggressive` flag's deeper delta compression. This points to the prune window as the main tuning knob.

## Regarding a PR

Your proposed solutions in the issue align well with what the code analysis suggests. Reducing the prune window (from `7.days` to something shorter) would have the most impact for the least complexity. A size-based trigger for more aggressive gc would handle the edge cases.

The one thing already in place that your proposal assumes is missing: the hourly `git gc` does already run. So the PR scope would be about **tuning the existing gc parameters** rather than adding gc from scratch.

title: MCP server "context7" restart loop (KiloCode treats stderr as failure / Not connected)
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

5.8.1

### Description

# Issue draft: MCP server "context7" restart loop (KiloCode treats stderr as failure / Not connected)

## Title
MCP server "context7" stuck in reconnect/restart loop: "Not connected" after reading stderr startup banner (stdio transport)

## Repo
https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode

## Background / Related
I found an existing issue and fix PR that look related:
- Issue: MCPs stuck in a restart loop after settings changes #5781
- PR: fix: prevent MCP servers from restarting on every settings file re-read #5897 (merged)

My case is still reproducible on Windows even after reinstalling VS Code + KiloCode.

## Environment
- OS: Windows 11
- VS Code: 1.109.5 (commit 072586267e68ece9a47aa43f8c108e0dcbf44622) x64
- KiloCode extension: 5.8.1
- MCP server: @upstash/context7-mcp v2.1.1
- Transport: stdio
- Node: v22.16.0
- npx: 10.9.2

## Symptoms
KiloCode continuously shows MCP server connected / restarting (loop). In logs it repeatedly reports:
- `Failed to connect to new MCP server context7: Error: Not connected`
- `Invalid configuration for MCP server "context7": Error: Not connected`

## Evidence (logs)
From VS Code log `window1/renderer.log`:

1) context7 prints startup banner/warning to stderr:
- `Server "context7" stderr: WARNING: Using default CLIENT_IP_ENCRYPTION_KEY.`
- `Server "context7" stderr: Context7 Documentation MCP Server v2.1.1 running on stdio`

2) Immediately followed by KiloCode connection failure and restart loop:
- `Failed to connect to new MCP server context7: Error: Not connected`
- then repeated `restartConnection(...)`

Additionally, KiloCode sometimes logs path handling errors:
- `RangeError: path should be a path.relative()d string, but got "../.kilocode/mcp.json"`
- same for `../.kilocode/skills`

## Expected
KiloCode should accept stderr output from MCP servers (or treat it as informational), and only fail on non-zero exit / handshake timeout / invalid JSON-RPC.

## Actual
When the MCP server writes a startup banner/warning to stderr (even on success), KiloCode logs it as error and ends up in `Not connected` state, triggering restart loop.

## Workaround
Wrap the MCP server with a small proxy that:
- pipes stdin/stdout for JSON-RPC
- silences or redirects stderr

Example wrapper (Windows-safe):

```js
// node wrapper: run "npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp --transport stdio" via cmd.exe, pipe stdio, drop stderr
// (full implementation available on request)
```

This stops the restart loop because KiloCode no longer sees stderr output.

## Ask
- Please confirm whether KiloCode currently treats any stderr output from MCP servers as fatal.
- Fix suggestion: do not mark connection as failed based solely on stderr; preserve stderr logs but keep the transport alive.
- Also please address the `path.relative()` RangeError when checking protection for paths like `C:\Users\...\.kilocode\mcp.json`.


### Reproduction steps

How can we trigger this bug? Clear steps:

1. **Fresh install / clean state (optional but helps)**
   - Ensure VS Code and Kilo Code extension are installed.
   - (Optional) Clear Kilo Code state by uninstalling/reinstalling the extension.

2. **Configure a stdio MCP server that writes a startup banner to stderr**
   - Open Kilo Code MCP settings file:
     - `C:\Users\<YOU>\AppData\Roaming\Code\User\globalStorage\kilocode.kilo-code\settings\mcp_settings.json`
   - Set config to:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "context7": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@upstash/context7-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

3. **Reload window**
   - Run VS Code command: `Developer: Reload Window`.

4. **Trigger MCP connection**
   - Open Kilo Code sidebar / Agent Manager so that Kilo Code initializes MCP servers.

5. **Observe the loop**
   - Kilo Code repeatedly shows the MCP server connected / restarting.
   - Open VS Code logs (Help → Toggle Developer Tools / or inspect log files) and check `.../Code/logs/<date>/window1/renderer.log`.
   - You should see repeats of:
     - `Server "context7" stderr: Context7 Documentation MCP Server ... running on stdio`
     - immediately followed by `Failed to connect ... Error: Not connected` and repeated `restartConnection(...)`.

6. **Control / confirmation**
   - Replace the MCP command with a wrapper that silences `stderr` while piping stdin/stdout.
   - Reload window again.
   - The restart loop stops, confirming `stderr` handling is part of the trigger.

### Provider

-

### Model

any

### System Information

Windows 11

title: [Feature]: Auto-generate CLI reference docs from help.ts
body:
## Summary

Use `generateHelp` from `src/kilocode/help.ts` to auto-generate CLI reference documentation, eliminating manual maintenance of the command table in `cli.md` and adding a full detailed reference page.

## Problem

`packages/kilo-docs/pages/code-with-ai/platforms/cli.md` contains a hand-written command table that is already stale — lists `kilo github` (disabled), missing `acp` and `help`. Every command add/remove/rename requires a manual docs update that is easy to forget.

## Design

Two generated artifacts, one source of truth:

1. **Quick reference table** — a Markdoc partial (`cli-commands-table.md`) included in the existing CLI docs page via `{% partial %}`. Replaces the hand-written table.
2. **Full CLI reference page** — a standalone page (`cli-reference.md`) with detailed help for every command and subcommand, generated by `generateHelp({ all: true, format: "md" })`.

Both are generated by `script/generate.ts` and auto-committed by the existing `generate.yml` workflow on push to `dev`.

## Depends on

- `kilo help --all` feature (PR Kilo-Org/kilo#571 / issue Kilo-Org/kilocode#6294)

## Status

🚧 **Work in progress** on branch `feat/help-all-command`

title: Severe performance degradation, extremely slow API calls and orchestration planning taking 20–30 minutes
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

5.7.0

### Description

Over the past weeks, I have experienced a significant performance degradation when using the Kilo Code extension inside Antigravity IDE.

Previously, the system was fast and reliable. However, it has gradually become almost unusable.

Main issues
API calls frequently take several minutes to complete
Orchestrator planning tasks take approximately 20–30 minutes to finish
Frequent API call failures and limit errors, even when not using the Kilo Gateway
Inconsistent performance when using my own AI Gateway — sometimes slightly better, sometimes equally slow
This happens regardless of the model used (Minimax, GLM-5, etc.)

At the moment, I have been trying to complete a single orchestration plan for 4 days, which is not normal and was never the case before.

Troubleshooting already attempted
I have already tried Clearing old conversations, closing unused files, restarting Antigravity, switching models, switching between Kilo Gateway and my own AI Gateway
None of these actions resolved the issue.

Planning and orchestration should complete within a reasonable time frame (as it did before), and API calls should respond consistently without frequent failures.

I have also spoken with several colleagues and friends using Kilo Code, and they report the same issue.
They describe that Kilo Code has become extremely heavy on their machines, with very slow responses, high latency, and overall degraded performance compared to before.

Is this a known issue?
Any guidance or debugging steps would be greatly appreciated.

### Reproduction steps

1. Open Antigravity IDE
2. Open any existing project (medium or large size, in my case a web app project)
3. Open Kilo Code extension
4. Select any model (tested with Minimax and GLM-5)
5. Use Orchestrator mode
6. Request a planning task, for example:
7. Create a new feature plan
8. Refactor an existing module
9. Generate an implementation plan
10. Wait for the planning process to start

### Provider

Kilo Code Gataway, Z.ai API, Open AI Plus account

### Model

GLM5, Minimax M2.5, Codex

### System Information

Operating System: Windows 11 Pro 64-bit
Hardware: Samsung Galaxy Book5 Pro
CPU: Intel Core Ultra processor (Series 2)
RAM: 16GB
1T SSD
Graphics: Intel Arc integrated graphics
Architecture: x64

title: [FEATURE]: Learn Mode agent for anti-cognitive offloading
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

AI coding agents ship code fast, but developers stop understanding what they ship. When the AI builds a feature, the developer often can't explain how it works, debug it when it breaks, or extend it confidently. This creates invisible technical debt -- not in the codebase, but in the developer's brain.

### Describe the solution you'd like

A native **Learn Mode** agent that turns AI-assisted coding into AI-mentored coding:

- Implements code fully (no degraded experience)
- Pauses after implementation to ask 2-3 comprehension questions referencing real identifiers from the code just written
- Tracks understanding with a persistent learning tracker (4 question categories: Comprehension, Reasoning, System Thinking, Edge Cases)
- Auto-calibrates difficulty via a sliding window algorithm over the last 5 answers
- Persists progress across sessions via a project-scoped aggregate
- Shows real-time level + check count in the TUI prompt bar

### Describe alternatives you've considered

1. **External quiz tools** -- Separate from the coding workflow, developers won't use them
2. **Code comments only** -- Static, don't verify understanding
3. **Post-session reviews** -- Too late, context is lost

### Additional context

Full implementation available in PR Kilo-Org/kilo#537, spanning:
- Native agent registration with custom permissions
- `learnwrite`/`learnread` tools with permission gating
- Learning tracker with Zod schemas, Storage persistence, Bus events
- Server routes (`GET /session/:sessionID/learn`, `GET /session/learn-aggregate`)
- TUI prompt bar indicator via SSE
- 34 tests covering tracker, tools, calibration, and aggregate

### Verification

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before

title: The "Error details" popup should show the x-request-id  HTTP response header - to debug requests
body: ### Plugin Type JetBrains Plugin (probably VScode too) ### App Version 5.8 ### Description From time to time Kilo shows such "Error details" popup: There are lot of info in this pop-up - BUT - one very important part is missing: the ``` x-request-id: Unique identifier for this API request (used in troubleshooting) ``` (see documentation https://developers.openai.com/api/reference/overview#debugging-requests ) Please **show the x-request-id HTTP response header in "Error details" popup!** When showing detailed error info - show ALL HTTP response headers - all of them! ### Reproduction steps 1. Somewhere middle in some workflow Kilo shows ``` Provider Error Unknown API error, click Details for more information.Details ``` 2. I am clicking "Details" and see "Error details" dialog ### Provider _No response_ ### Model Kimi K2.5 ### System Information _No response_
author: stargazer33

comments.author: peterkc
comments.body:
Tracing through the codebase, the response headers are already captured and propagated through the error chain — they just aren't surfaced in the UI.

## Current Data Flow

The `responseHeaders` from failed API calls already travel end-to-end:

1. The Vercel AI SDK's `APICallError` captures HTTP response headers (including `x-request-id`) from the upstream provider
2. [`ProviderError.parseAPICallError()`](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/blob/main/packages/opencode/src/provider/error.ts#L184) passes them into the parsed error object
3. [`MessageV2.APIError`](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/blob/main/packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts#L42) stores `responseHeaders` as `Record<string, string>`
4. The [SDK `ApiError` type](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/blob/main/packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts#L201) exposes `responseHeaders` to all client surfaces

The retry system already reads these headers (e.g., `retry-after-ms` in `retry.ts`), confirming the data is live and populated.

## The Gap

The error display in [`session-turn.tsx`](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/blob/main/packages/ui/src/components/session-turn.tsx#L236-L241) only renders `error.data.message`:

```tsx
const errorText = createMemo(() => {
  const msg = error()?.data?.message
  // ...
})
// ...
<Card variant="error" class="error-card">
  {errorText()}
</Card>
```

The `responseHeaders`, `responseBody`, `statusCode`, and `metadata` fields are all present in the error object but not displayed.

## Scope

The shared `session-turn.tsx` in `@opencode-ai/ui` is used by the Desktop app, CLI TUI, and JetBrains plugin — a fix there would cover those three surfaces.

The VSCode extension has its own webview rendering (`packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/`) independent of the shared UI package, so it would need a parallel update to surface these fields.

In both cases, `ApiError.data.responseHeaders` is already available — the work is purely on the UI side, expanding the error card to show additional details (headers, status code, response body) in a collapsible or expandable section.

title: [FEATURE]: Windows Explorer context-menu install/uninstall for Kilo CLI
body:
> **Kilo League Challenge Kilo-Org/kilo#3 — DeveloperWeek 2026 Hackathon Entry**

### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

Add an official Windows Explorer context-menu integration for Kilo CLI so users can right-click any folder (or folder background, or drive) and select **"Open Kilo CLI Here"** to launch Kilo scoped to that location.

#### The Problem

Windows users currently need to `cd` into a directory before running `kilo`. There's no native OS integration — you can't right-click a folder and jump straight into a Kilo session. VS Code, Windows Terminal, and Git Bash all offer this; Kilo should too.

#### Proposed Solution

Two built-in commands:

| Command | Effect |
|---------|--------|
| `kilo windows install-context-menu` | Registers per-user context-menu entries (HKCU — no admin required) |
| `kilo windows remove-context-menu` | Cleanly removes all entries |

**Registry targets (per-user, no elevation):**

| Context | Registry Key |
|---------|-------------|
| Folder background | `HKCU\Software\Classes\Directory\Background\shell\Kilo` |
| Folder | `HKCU\Software\Classes\Directory\shell\Kilo` |
| Drive | `HKCU\Software\Classes\Drive\shell\Kilo` |

Each entry gets:
- Label: **"Open Kilo CLI Here"**
- Icon: `kilo.exe` embedded icon
- Command: `kilo "%V"` (passes clicked path)

#### Why This Matters

- **Zero-friction onboarding** — new Windows users get native OS integration immediately
- **No admin required** — HKCU keys work without elevation, safe for corporate machines
- **Clean uninstall** — `remove-context-menu` leaves no registry residue
- **Precedent** — VS Code, Git Bash, Windows Terminal all do this; Kilo should match

I tested this locally and it's a strong quality-of-life improvement for Windows workflows.

body: ## Description Some thinking models like `kimi-k2-thinking` fail when invoked via the Kilo Gateway with the error: ``` Reasoning is mandatory for this endpoint and cannot be disabled. ``` This affects: - `kimi-k2-thinking` (and potentially other `-thinking` suffix models) via `@kilocode/kilo-gateway` ## Expected Behavior Thinking models should work out of the box without requiring manual reasoning toggle. ## Root Cause The `ProviderTransform.options()` function in `packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts` was not adding `reasoning: { enabled: true }` for thinking models accessed via Kilo Gateway. ## Fix Add logic to automatically enable reasoning for models that: 1. title: Function call is missing a thought_signature model Gemini 3.1 Pro
body:
### Plugin Type

JetBrains Plugin

### App Version

5.8.0

### Description

Date/time: 2026-02-19T23:17:35.072Z
Extension version: 5.8.0
Provider: gemini
Model: gemini-3.1-pro-preview

Gemini generate context stream error: {"error":{"message":"{\n  \"error\": {\n    \"code\": 400,\n    \"message\": \"Function call is missing a thought_signature in functionCall parts. This is required for tools to work correctly, and missing thought_signature may lead to degraded model performance. Additional data, function call `default_api:read_file` , position 2. Please refer to https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/thought-signatures for more details.\",\n    \"status\": \"INVALID_ARGUMENT\"\n  }\n}\n","code":400,"status":"Bad Request"}}

### Reproduction steps

- any prompt to gemini 3.1-pro

### Provider

Gemini

### Model

gemini-3.1-pro-preview

### System Information

MacOS
author: mayeco

comments.author: PoCk3T
comments.body: Same problem in the VS Code extension

comments.author: newcommit
comments.body: Same issue

comments.author: sulimma
comments.body: This also happens for gemini-3.1-pro-preview-customtools

comments.author: peterkc
comments.body:
## Diagnostic Analysis

I investigated the full message pipeline to understand where `thoughtSignature` is lost:

**Pipeline**: `processor.ts` (capture) → SQLite (storage) → `message-v2.ts:toModelMessages()` (replay) → AI SDK `convertToModelMessages` → `@ai-sdk/google` converter → Gemini API

### Findings

1. **Unit tests**: `toModelMessages()` correctly preserves `providerMetadata` (containing `thoughtSignature`) for same-model Gemini sessions. The `differentModel` guard at line 600 evaluates `false` when models match, and metadata flows through.

2. **E2E test**: Ran a live tool-call against `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` using `@ai-sdk/google@2.0.54`. The SDK emits `thoughtSignature` in `providerMetadata` on every `tool-call` event, and the response message includes it in `providerOptions.google.thoughtSignature`. Full round-trip works.

3. **Root cause (likely resolved)**: Commit `021e42c0b` added the `differentModel` guard to strip cross-provider metadata. The original comparison used `model.api.id` on one side but `msg.info.modelID` (set from `model.id`) on the other — a mismatch that could cause `differentModel` to evaluate `true` for same-model Gemini sessions, stripping the `thoughtSignature` metadata.

### This appears fixed in v5.9.0

The JetBrains plugin [v5.9.0 release notes](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/28350-kilo-code/versions/stable/963706) include:

> fix: preserve extra_content for Gemini 3 thought_signature support

The issue was filed against v5.8.0. **Could you update to v5.9.0 and verify whether the issue persists?**

If it does, I have diagnostic tests and an E2E tracer ready to dig deeper. I've also opened #6368 to add regression test coverage for Gemini `thoughtSignature` metadata preservation — this scenario had zero test coverage before.

Please check #6203 for more details and any workarounds. If your situation differs, please comment on that issue with additional context.

title: Disk space leak: orphaned tmp_pack_* files from incomplete git repack operations
body:
## Bug Description

Kilo accumulates orphaned `tmp_pack_*` files in the snapshot directory when git repack operations are interrupted or fail. These temporary files are never cleaned up, causing significant disk space consumption over time.

In my case, I discovered approximately **20GB** of orphaned temporary pack files:

```
$ ls -lah ~/.local/share/kilo/snapshot/global/objects/pack/
total 20G
-r--r--r-- 8.7G Feb 19 16:54 tmp_pack_51Fkud
-r--r--r--  11G Feb 19 17:12 tmp_pack_mZvL5A
```

The total snapshot directory grew to **52GB** due to these orphaned files.

## Root Cause

Git creates temporary files with the pattern `tmp_pack_*` during repack operations. If the repack is interrupted (crash, timeout, kill signal, etc.), these files remain in the objects/pack directory. Kilo's snapshot mechanism appears to trigger repacks but doesn't handle cleanup of these orphaned temp files after failures.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Use Kilo extensively (snapshot operations accumulate)
2. Interrupt a repack operation (system crash, process killed, timeout, etc.)
3. Check `~/.local/share/kilo/snapshot/*/objects/pack/` for `tmp_pack_*` files

## Environment

- OS: Linux
- Kilo version: Latest
- Snapshot directory size: 52GB (20GB from orphaned temp files)

## Proposed Solution

Implement cleanup of orphaned `tmp_pack_*` files:

1. **On startup**: Scan for and remove any `tmp_pack_*` files older than a threshold (e.g., files created before the current process started)
2. **After repack**: Ensure cleanup runs even if repack fails (try/finally or equivalent)
3. **Periodic maintenance**: Add a maintenance routine that removes stale temp files

Git itself considers these files safe to delete if no git process is using them (they're temp files from incomplete operations).

## Offer to Help

I'd be happy to implement this fix. I can:
- Add cleanup logic to the snapshot management code
- Add tests for the cleanup functionality
- Document the maintenance behavior

Please let me know if this approach sounds reasonable and I'll submit a PR.
author: mehrdadbn9

This issue might be a duplicate of an existing issue. Please check:
- Kilo-Org/kilocode#6302: [Orphaned git pack files from incomplete repack operations cause disk space leak](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/issues/6302)

Both issues describe the same problem with orphaned `tmp_pack_*` files consuming significant disk space. If this is a duplicate, the team will likely consolidate discussion into one issue.

title: Windows ARM64 incompatible
body:
### Description

Installed the cli as per guidelines, but looks like win-arm64 version is not included in built releases (though is available for macos and linux)

```
PowerShell 7.5.4
PS C:\Users\nos1609> kilo
It seems that your package manager failed to install the right version of the Kilo CLI for your platform. You can try manually installing the "@kilocode/cli-windows-arm64" package
PS C:\Users\nos1609> npm install -g @kilocode/cli-windows-arm64
npm notice Access token expired or revoked. Please try logging in again.
npm error code E404
npm error 404 Not Found - GET https://registry.npmjs.org/@kilocode%2fcli-windows-arm64 - Not found
npm error 404
npm error 404  The requested resource '@kilocode/cli-windows-arm64@*' could not be found or you do not have permission to access it.
```



Native installed Node.js on an X-Elite Copilot+PC (works perfectly with codex, claude, gemini, kwen)
```
npm -v
11.8.0
node -v
v24.13.1
```


### Plugins

none (yet)

1.0.23

1. npm install -g @kilocode/cli
2. npm install -g @kilocode/cli-windows-arm64

### Screenshot and/or share link

_No response_

### Operating System

Windows 11 25H2

### Terminal

Windows Terminal or VS Code terminal
author: nos1609

title: [FEATURE] Help user discover their provider/model pair availability
body:
## Summary

I am missing an easy way to _check in with each of the models_ listed by `kilo models` to see which ones are _actually_ available via API for me to use, today, _now_. 

I think of this action as a "[roll call](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MADs1hhwpE8)", like the one you do in the morning at [school](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_u6OYuiTJsc) (_or in prison, eheh_) to check for any absence. 

I have actually been doing this in the past with a bash `for`-loop and a lot of `opencode run`, but that way is _painfully slow_ for obvious reasons - launching the CLI from scratch a several, potentially a hundred times, new sessions, managing timeouts ... and then on top of that, I have to parse the somewhat unstructured output of my survey! In short, there ought to be a built-in way to perform this type of survey/diagnostic on behalf of the user if they need to. The plugin interface does not expose the service necessary to do this, nor does it support adding sub-commands, so a new feature is in order.

## Proposed Solution

Add a `roll-call` sub-command that iterates through configured provider/model pairs and reports their availability status (or make it a _sub-sub_ command like "`kilo models test [...]`", whatever is best from a discoverability/usability standpoint). 

Example use case: "I want need to test a certain prompt against all "_gwen_" models". 

Finally, this is also a good opportunity to measure real-world latency.

title: [FEATURE]: Warm Agents — deterministic orchestration with stateful agent reuse
body:
## Summary

Introduce a **Warm Agents** orchestration layer that adds deterministic dispatch, structured warmness scoring, and safety interlocks to Kilo's existing multi-agent system.

### Current State — What Kilo Already Does Well

Kilo's `task` tool already supports **session-level agent reuse** via the `task_id` parameter. When an orchestrator passes a prior `task_id`, the child session is resumed with its full conversation history intact — files read, tools called, decisions made. This is meaningful context reuse driven by the orchestrator's prompt-level judgment.

The orchestrator also supports **wave-based parallelism**: independent subtasks are issued as parallel tool calls in a single LLM message, with dependent work sequenced across waves.

### The Gap — What Warm Agents Adds

The current reuse is **prompt-driven** (the orchestrator decides) rather than **state-driven** (the system knows). Warm Agents fills four specific gaps:

| Current Behavior | With Warm Agents |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| Orchestrator manually decides which `task_id` to resume | **Warmness scorer** evaluates all candidates and routes to the best-prepared agent automatically |
| Context reuse is locked to one child session | **Cross-session warmness** — a new task benefits from what a sibling agent learned about the same files |
| No visibility into what a child session "knows" without replaying its full chat | **Queryable warmness snapshots** — loaded files, tool history, project scope persisted as structured data |
| No enforcement of mutation scope | **Blast-radius declarations** with invariant middleware blocking out-of-scope tool calls |
| Step-level snapshots exist, but no task-level rollback | **Task-level postcondition checks** with automatic rollback to pre-task state on failure |
| No structured record of dispatch decisions | **Append-only audit log** answering: what was it trying to do, what could it change, what survives process death |

### Core Capabilities

1. **Warmness-scored agent dispatch** — a composite scorer (recency 20%, file familiarity 35%, tool match 20%, task continuity 25%) routes work to the best-prepared agent. Extends — not replaces — the existing `task_id` resumption.

2. **Typed lifecycle state machines** — explicit states for agents (`cold → warming → warm → executing → cooling`) and tasks (`pending → claimed → executing → postchecked → completed | failed | rolled_back`), all persisted to durable storage.

3. **Blast-radius declarations** — every task declares what files/operations/tools it may touch. An invariant middleware enforces this at the tool-call level, blocking out-of-scope mutations before they execute.

4. **Postcondition checks + automatic rollback** — after task execution, postconditions are verified. Failures in `--auto` mode trigger snapshot-based rollback to pre-task state.

5. **Append-only audit log** — every dispatch decision, state transition, invariant check, and rollback is recorded in JSONL format.

6. **MCP lifecycle awareness** — periodic health checks, tool schema drift detection, and routing fallback when MCP servers degrade.

### Design Principles

- **Additive seams, not invasive rewrites** — all new code lives in `packages/opencode/src/warm/`. Only 3 existing files gain optional integration points.
- **Opt-in via `--warm` flag** — zero behavior change without the flag.
- **Extends existing systems** — builds on `Storage`, `Snapshot`, `PermissionNext`, `Bus`, and the existing `task_id` session reuse rather than replacing them.
- **Consistent patterns** — dispatch rules use the same last-wins evaluation as `PermissionNext`.

### Implementation Phases

| Phase | Scope |
|-------|-------|
| **Prototype** | Schemas, scorer, audit log, single-agent warmness tracking |
| **Integration** | Multi-agent dispatch, invariant middleware, rollback, `--warm` flag |
| **Hardening** | MCP health, replay engine, `--auto` safety, crash recovery |

### Motivation

This contribution targets the **"For Devs, By Devs"** track of Kilo League Challenge Kilo-Org/kilo#3 / DeveloperWeek 2026 hackathon — improving Kilo's orchestration reliability for unattended and CI/CD use cases.

### Architecture Document

Full architecture proposal with component diagrams, Zod schemas, state machines, routing policies, safety harness design, and acceptance criteria is available in the PR branch at `docs/warm-agents-architecture.md`.

title: Kilo CLI uses Linux commands in tool calls when running on Windows
body:
### Description

I'm running Kilo CLI (installed via npm) on Windows 11. Using the Orchestrator agent, I'm giving it a prompt to implement a feature. In the various sub-agents that Orchestrator launches, it is performing command-line tool calls using `bash` which looks like it is actually launching WSL since that's what `bash` at the Windows command-line launches.

In this particular example, it executed `dir "C:\Path\To\My\Project" /a` (with backslashes) which failed with: `/usr/bin/bash: -c: line 1: unexpected EOF while looking for matching `"'`. It then switched to using `ls -la` but still with a Windows path using forward slashes, which just happens to work in WSL.

### Plugins

_No response_

### Kilo version

Kilo CLI 1.0.23

### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch Kilo CLI on Windows.
2. Give a prompt to the Orchestrator agent that requires inspecting and modifying files.

### Screenshot and/or share link

_No response_

### Operating System

Windows 11

### Terminal

Windows Terminal
author: doxxx

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
This issue appears to be related to the broader Windows path handling issues tracked in Kilo-Org/kilocode#6332. The problems you're experiencing with backslashes and path escaping in bash tool calls are part of a larger set of Windows filepath challenges documented there.

Please check Kilo-Org/kilocode#6332 to see if your issue is already being addressed as part of that effort, and feel free to add any additional details or test cases that might help resolve the Windows path handling problems.

comments.author: doxxx
comments.body: I disagree that it's simply about Windows file paths. It shouldn't be executing `bash` to run shell commands on Windows.

comments.author: doxxx
comments.body: It's also wasting a lot of tokens on these errors and various syntax errors as it tries different commands.

comments.author: doxxx
comments.body: One more data point: I'm using Kilo CLI with my Github Copilot subscription using the Claude Sonnet 4.6 model.

comments.author: doxxx
comments.body: It's also creating `nul` files in the root of my workspace. Most likely because it's trying execute Windows-like commands, e.g. `dir some\path 2>nul` which would redirect errors to the NUL device but because it's using `bash` to execute the command, WSL doesn't know about the Windows NUL device and just creates a `nul` file with the redirected output.

comments.author: doxxx
comments.body: Actually, it looks like it's running Git Bash? The commands it was trying succeed (mostly) in Git Bash but not in WSL. However, it looks like it was trying to run CMD commands (e.g. `dir C;\path\to\repo /a 2>nul`). While Git Bash has a `dir` executable that does the right thing, the redirect to the NUL device is not correct for Git Bash.

comments.author: pschiel
comments.body:
the CLI needs a shell to run commands, on Windows this can be:
- Git Bash (preferred way, native, comes with install Git for Windows)
- WSL Bash (but WSL not compatible with Windows paths and other issues, similar to Docker)
- cmd.exe or powershell/pwsh

If you run Git Bash and the agent is trying to run "dir" commands, it's most likely due to the "Platform: win32" environment info from the system prompt being a bit vague.

You can fix this by adding instructions in your AGENTS.md, that you're running a Git Bash on Windows, and to avoid backslashes in paths.

A few other path issues need https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilo/pull/108 to fix.

If you still absolutely want cmd.exe or pwsh (while having git bash or wsl installed), you can override `SHELL` and `KILO_GIT_BASH_PATH` environment variables to point to their absolute paths

comments.author: doxxx
comments.body: IMO, Kilo CLI should use the native tools for the platform, i.e. PowerShell on Windows, bash on Linux/macOS. Depending on having Git Bash installed seems a little sketchy. I'd also argue that it's Kilo CLI's responsibility to give the necessary system instructions so that the model knows what platform and shell it should be generating commands for.

comments.author: pschiel
comments.body:
> IMO, Kilo CLI should use the native tools for the platform, i.e. PowerShell on Windows, bash on Linux/macOS. Depending on having Git Bash installed seems a little sketchy. I'd also argue that it's Kilo CLI's responsibility to give the necessary system instructions so that the model knows what platform and shell it should be generating commands for.

Opencode and Kilo are hard depending on git for a lot of functions, Git Bash isn't sketchy it's the default. If you want to argue and insist on backslashes and cmd.exe - have fun. You will run into dozens of bugs

comments.author: doxxx
comments.body:
I'm not arguing for cmd.exe -- it's a deadend. That's why I suggested PowerShell. And it handles forward slashes in paths just fine.

Regardless of what shell it's using, the model is not being prompted correctly and so it's flailing around generating the wrong commands for the shell and platform. I shouldn't have to tell it in my prompt or instruction files what operating system and shell it's generatintg commands for. The agent framework (i.e. Kilo CLI) should be doing that.

comments.author: doxxx
comments.body: Also, my point about depending on Git Bash being installed is that it is a perfectly valid scenario to _not_ have Git Bash installed and having Kilo CLI be broken in that scenario just seems be a poor design decision.

comments.author: pschiel
comments.body:
> Also, my point about depending on Git Bash being installed is that it is a perfectly valid scenario to _not_ have Git Bash installed and having Kilo CLI be broken in that scenario just seems be a poor design decision.

Of course that's fine. Your concrete problem was that your CLI uses git bash currently, but the agent tried to run cmd.exe commands, having only the info that it runs on win32.

This patch should help, it adds the path of the current used shell to the environment system prompt: https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilo/pull/573

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
This issue might be a duplicate of or related to existing issues. Please check:
- #6099: Windows path issues (blocking, kilo-triaged) - covers broader Windows path handling including bash tool issues
- #6332: Windows path issues - similar to #6099

Your specific case about bash commands using Windows paths in WSL appears to be one manifestation of the broader Windows path handling problems already being tracked.

title: Add font size setting for webview UI
body:
## Problem

Currently, users cannot customize the font size in the Kilo Code webview UI. The font size is fixed at 13px, which may not be suitable for all users, especially those with visual impairments or different screen resolutions.

## Proposed Solution

Add a font size setting in the Display settings tab that allows users to adjust the webview UI font size from 10px to 24px (default: 13px).

## Implementation

- Add `fontSize` config option (range: 10-24, default: 13)
- Add slider in Settings > Display tab
- Apply font size via CSS variables (`--font-size-small`, `--font-size-base`, etc.)
- Persist setting in global config

## Related

Similar to issue #2607 in Kilo-Org/kilocode (old repo) for VS Code text size.

title: Mouse scroll events should scroll messages panel, not input history
body:
## Problem

When using the Kilo CLI TUI, mouse scroll wheel events are routed to the currently focused component. Since the input textarea maintains focus, scroll events are interpreted as up/down navigation, which cycles through command history instead of scrolling the messages panel.

This is unexpected behavior - users naturally expect scroll wheel to scroll the main content area (messages), not navigate through command history in the input box.

## Current Behavior

1. User scrolls mouse wheel
2. Scroll event goes to input textarea (has focus)
3. Up/down events cycle through command history
4. Messages panel does not scroll

## Expected Behavior

1. User scrolls mouse wheel
2. Scroll event scrolls the messages panel
3. Input textarea focus is preserved for typing
4. Command history navigation remains available via up/down arrow keys

## Suggested Solution

Add a configuration option to route scroll events to the messages scrollbox regardless of input focus. This could be:

```json
{
  "tui": {
    "scroll_routing": "messages"
  }
}
```

Possible values:
- `"focus"` (default) - current behavior, scroll goes to focused component
- `"messages"` - always route scroll to messages panel

Alternatively, the input textarea could simply ignore scroll events since:
1. Multi-line input is limited to 6 lines (minimal need for scroll)
2. Up/down are already used for command history navigation
3. Users can use Ctrl+arrow keys for line navigation within multi-line input

## Related Code

- `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/routes/session/index.tsx` - Main session component with scrollbox
- `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/component/prompt/index.tsx` - Input/prompt component
- OpenTUI native Zig renderer handles mouse event routing

## Workaround

Users can use Page Up/Down or Ctrl+Alt+U/D to scroll messages, but this is not intuitive for mouse users.

title: Figma MCP Remote Server fails to connect (OAuth / streamableHttp not working)
body:
### Plugin Type

JetBrains Plugin

### App Version

last version

### Description

I attempted to configure the official **Figma MCP Remote Server** in Kilo Code based on the official documentation:

👉 [https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/remote-server-installation/](https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/remote-server-installation/)

However, Kilo Code fails to connect to the server. I tested multiple configurations, but none worked.

---

## 🔍 Findings from Figma Official Documentation

According to the official docs, the remote server:

* Uses **HTTP / streamableHttp transport** (NOT SSE)
* Requires **OAuth authentication**
* Does NOT support API keys via headers
* Should open a browser window for user authorization

---

## ❌ Problems Encountered

### 1. Incorrect transport behavior

Using `"type": "sse"` results in a **502 error**, which aligns with the docs stating SSE is not supported.

---

### 2. OAuth flow does not trigger

When using the correct transport type (`http` or `streamableHttp`), **no OAuth browser window opens**, so authentication cannot complete.

---

### 3. Headers / API token do not work

Adding `x-figma-token` headers has no effect, which matches the documentation (OAuth required).

---

## ✅ Configurations Tested

### Configuration A — streamableHttp (recommended by docs)

```json
"figma": {
  "type": "streamableHttp",
  "url": "https://mcp.figma.com/mcp",
  "alwaysAllow": []
}
```

**Result:** Connection fails / no OAuth flow

---

### Configuration B — http transport

```json
"figma": {
  "type": "http",
  "url": "https://mcp.figma.com/mcp",
  "alwaysAllow": []
}
```

**Result:** Same failure — no authentication prompt

---

### Configuration C — SSE (legacy attempt)

```json
"type": "sse"
```

**Result:** 502 error

---

## 🧪 Alternative Test (Unofficial MCP)

Using the unofficial `figma-developer-mcp` package with a Personal Access Token works correctly:

```json
"figma": {
  "command": "npx",
  "args": ["-y", "figma-developer-mcp", "--figma-api-key=XXXX"],
  "alwaysAllow": []
}
```

This suggests the issue is specific to OAuth-based remote MCP support.

---

## ❓ Expected Behavior

When starting the MCP server:

1. Browser should open automatically
2. User should authorize Figma access
3. Kilo Code should display something like:

```
Authentication successful. Connected to figma
```

---

## 📌 Actual Behavior

* No browser window opens
* Authentication never occurs
* Connection fails or hangs

---

## 💡 Possible Cause

Kilo Code may not yet support:

* `streamableHttp` transport
* OAuth-based remote MCP servers
* Automatic browser-based authorization flow

---

## 🖥️ Environment

* Tool: Kilo Code
* MCP Server: Figma Remote MCP (`https://mcp.figma.com/mcp`)

### Reproduction steps

## 🙏 Request

Please confirm:

* Whether Figma Remote MCP servers are officially supported
* Whether OAuth flow is implemented for remote MCP connections
* Recommended configuration for connecting to Figma MCP

### Provider

_No response_

### Model

_No response_

### System Information

_No response_
