title: The originally generated code was deleted, but Kilo still claims that the deleted files exist and continuously attempts to read these non-existent files.
body:
### Plugin Type

JetBrains Plugin

### App Version

Version: 4.138.0 (2ce7c7fd)

### Description

The originally generated code was deleted, but Kilo still claims that the deleted files exist and continuously attempts to read these non-existent files.
The operations I performed:
1. Modified the character definition or changed the prompt words.
2. Deleted files originally generated by AI or those that were not generated by AI, but the AI still believes these files exist and continues to attempt to read them even though they no longer exist.
3. Although I modified the prompt words and the structure of the generated code is completely different from the original, the AI seems to have some kind of cache and continues to produce content based on the structure of the previously deleted files. It's as if it cannot clearly remember and keeps using outdated information that has been rendered useless, continuously affecting current generation tasks.
4. I cleared the cache of IntelliJ IDEA and deleted all Kilo conversations, but the problem persists.
I wonder if there is a button that allows me to clear these caches to prevent them from affecting subsequent code generation.

### Reproduction steps

1. delete files
2. change promt
3. kilo code still read deleted files and The file structure still generates something similar to the old prompt, seemingly affected by caching.

### Provider

Kilo code

### Model

qwen-coder

_No response_

title: Not able to run any inline shell commands using installed binaries
body: ### Plugin Type VSCode Extension ### App Version 4.140.0 (e8c4323f) ### Description Any shell command is not able to find my installed tools/binaries, like npm or yarn or pnpm. ### Reproduction steps Ask kilocode to run `npm i` or `pnpm i`. It fails with: ``` 'npm' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. ``` Try selecting powershell and bash in the dropdown. Also try changing vscode default terminal to powershell and git bash. Still fails, cant find any bins. Manual commands work fine in vscode's terminal window, both powershell and git bash can see npm, pnpm and yarn. But when kilocode tries commands it cant see any installed tools/bins. ### Provider x ai ### Model grok 4 1 fast ### System Information OS: Windows 11 CPU: AMD RAM: 16GB
author: dwidge

comments.author: dwidge
comments.body: These options were enabled in settings: ``` Inherit environment variables Use inline terminal (recommended) ``` I disabled `Use inline terminal (recommended)` and now the bin commands (`pnpm i`) work. So maybe the inline terminal seems to not inherit PATH etc from vscode? Also when I disabled it, it showed 2 identical checkboxes for `Inherit environment variables`. They seem to be in sync, if I (un)checked one the other is (un)checked.

title: Add Code search MCP for Claude Code
body:
Your entire codebase as Claude's context
License Node.js Documentation VS Code Marketplace npm - core npm - mcp Twitter DeepWiki discord

Claude Context is an MCP plugin that adds semantic code search to Claude Code and other AI coding agents, giving them deep context from your entire codebase.

🧠 Your Entire Codebase as Context: Claude Context uses semantic search to find all relevant code from millions of lines. No multi-round discovery needed. It brings results straight into the Claude's context.

💰 Cost-Effective for Large Codebases: Instead of loading entire directories into Claude for every request, which can be very expensive, Claude Context efficiently stores your codebase in a vector database and only uses related code in context to keep your costs manageable.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f4a701f-8d8d-463c-a35b-bf24680f1a02)
author: sergey99si

title: add Spec Kit MCP
body:
https://github.com/github/spec-kit

🤔 What is Spec-Driven Development?
Spec-Driven Development flips the script on traditional software development. For decades, code has been king — specifications were just scaffolding we built and discarded once the "real work" of coding began. Spec-Driven Development changes this: specifications become executable, directly generating working implementations rather than just guiding them.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/52c28442-7851-4b99-9aab-d763e2aac23a)

title: Add Ref. / MCP
body:
Agentic search for exactly the right context
Ref's MCP tools are designed to match how models search while using as little context as possible to reduce [context rot](https://research.trychroma.com/context-rot). The goal is to find exactly the context your coding agent needs to be successful while using minimum tokens. Depending on the complexity of the prompt, LLM coding agents like Claude Code will typically do one or more searches and then choose a few resources to read in more depth.

For a simple query about Figma's Comment REST API it will make a couple calls to get exactly what it needs:

https://ref.tools/mcp

title: add MCP Chrome
body:
https://github.com/hangwin/mcp-chrome.git


Chrome MCP Server 🚀
Stars License: MIT TypeScript Chrome Extension Release

🌟 Turn your Chrome browser into your intelligent assistant - Let AI take control of your browser, transforming it into a powerful AI-controlled automation tool.

📖 Documentation: [English](https://github.com/hangwin/mcp-chrome/blob/master/README.md) | [中文](https://github.com/hangwin/mcp-chrome/blob/master/README_zh.md)

The project is still in its early stages and is under intensive development. More features, stability improvements, and other enhancements will follow.

🎯 What is Chrome MCP Server?
Chrome MCP Server is a Chrome extension-based Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that exposes your Chrome browser functionality to AI assistants like Claude, enabling complex browser automation, content analysis, and semantic search. Unlike traditional browser automation tools (like Playwright), Chrome MCP Server directly uses your daily Chrome browser, leveraging existing user habits, configurations, and login states, allowing various large models or chatbots to take control of your browser and truly become your everyday assistant.

✨ Core Features
😁 Chatbot/Model Agnostic: Let any LLM or chatbot client or agent you prefer automate your browser
⭐️ Use Your Original Browser: Seamlessly integrate with your existing browser environment (your configurations, login states, etc.)
💻 Fully Local: Pure local MCP server ensuring user privacy
🚄 Streamable HTTP: Streamable HTTP connection method
🏎 Cross-Tab: Cross-tab context
🧠 Semantic Search: Built-in vector database for intelligent browser tab content discovery
🔍 Smart Content Analysis: AI-powered text extraction and similarity matching
🌐 20+ Tools: Support for screenshots, network monitoring, interactive operations, bookmark management, browsing history, and 20+ other tools
🚀 SIMD-Accelerated AI: Custom WebAssembly SIMD optimization for 4-8x faster vector operations

title: Tasks hangup across workspaces when one of the uses .kilocode directory and the others donot
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

4.140

### Description

When you us Kilo agent with multiple workspaces/folders in VS code and one workspace adds specific behavioral instructions inside a `.kilocode` folder, other workspaces that do not have this folder will stop working.
The agent will become unresponsive and forced restarts result in `Task file not found` errors inside unrelated workspaces and projects that do not have a `.kilocode` folder or referenced rule files.

Similarly, rather than being workspace specific the Kilo history shows tasks across all workspaces (shared). 

There seem to be elements that are shared by default across workspaces, the easiest suggestions it to ensure that Kilo code works by default inside a given workspace rather than as sharing all configuration across them.



### Reproduction steps

1. INTITAL STATE: 
2. Create two folders A and B.
3. Use VS Code to "open folder" A and in second VS Code window "open folder" B, i.e. open two workspaces .
4. Create a simple task: e.g. "Create basic HTML page with red background." in each workspace/project. Observer working.
5. MODIFY Workspace A:
6. Create a `.kilocode` sub-folder in workspace/folder A. Further create `rules\coding-rules.md` inside this folder. 
7. Add `Tech Stack: React 19` as text inside the `coding-rules.md` file
8. Still inside workspace A, use the Kilo Rules panel  (little scale icon) and reference `coding-rules.md` as `Workspace Rules` file.
9. Do another task in Workspace A to confirm it is working, e.g. 'Create a HTML page that says HELLO MUNDO`
10.  FAILURE:
11. Open Workspace/Folder B
12. Try to do any task. --- the system will hang --> instead it could warn that no rule file is present or ignore non-existing rule files.

### Provider

ALL providers show same behavior, from Anthropic to Google, to Z.AI

### Model

Claude Sonnet 4

### System Information

OS: Windows 11
GPU: NVIDEA
CPU: Intel Core i7
RAM: 32GB

title: mcp_settings.json requires MCP server name longer than 3 characters to register in kilo
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

4.139.0 (8559957e)

### Description

Update: I discovered that the issue with registering the pal mcp server is related to the length of the server name. When I changed the server name from "pal" to "pals1" (making it longer than 3 characters), the MCP server registered correctly in kilo.

**Root cause:** The mcp server name must be longer than 3 characters to register successfully in kilo. Servers with names that are 3 characters or fewer do not register, even though they work in other agentic stacks.

**Proposed Resolution:**
- Update kilo to allow MCP server names that are three characters or fewer, or, at least document the name length requirement in the configuration documentation.

### Reproduction steps

1. Configure `mcp_settings.json` with the server name as "pal"
2. Observe that it does not register in kilo
3. Change the server name to something longer than 3 characters (e.g., "pals1")
4. Observe that it registers successfully

### Provider

title: Fix @vscode/policy-watcher compilation failure on macOS
body:
### Plugin Type

JetBrains Plugin

### App Version

4.137.0

### Description

The JetBrains plugin build fails on macOS when installing dependencies for `@vscode/policy-watcher`. The compilation error occurs because the `<optional>` header is missing from `PreferencesPolicy.hh`, causing `std::optional` to be undefined.

**Error Details:**
```
../src/macos/PreferencesPolicy.hh:64:10: error: no template named 'optional' in namespace 'std'
   64 |     std::optional<T> value;
       |     ~~~~~^
```

This prevents the JetBrains plugin from being built successfully on macOS systems.

### Reproduction steps

1. Clone the kilocode repository
2. Navigate to `jetbrains/plugin` directory
3. Run `pnpm run copy:resource-nodemodules` (or the full build command)
4. Observe compilation failure during `npm install` when building `@vscode/policy-watcher` native module



### Expected Behavior
The build should complete successfully without compilation errors.

### Actual Behavior
Build fails with C++ compilation errors related to missing `std::optional` definition.
```


### Provider

_No response_

title: Add more providers/models to autocomplete, and allow choice
body:
Currently we only support the codestral-2508 model for autocomplete. We have thoroughly tested this model with both the FIM endpoint which we complete and traditional chat-completions endpoint using the HoleFiller prompt.

We'd like to add more models but have to make sure these other models
1) work well
2) aren't expensive (as autocomplete does a lot of requests)

Therefore we probably do not want to allow full model choice like we do with chat, but probably something more similar to how we do it with Fast Apply.

To implement this we need (at least)

a) an interface to select from a limited set of providers/models
b) a procedure to test whether we deem the model good enough (probably using src/test-llm-completions, and some minimum requirements)

As for people who want this and find this issue, please tell us what models you'd want and why. We did try a lot of models before deciding on this one, and for instance grok-code-fast is free atm so it would seem like a good choice, but it is too slow to use for autocomplete.

title: Reasoning configuration not retain
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

4.137.0

### Description

When customizing the reasoning settings for a model, those settings are lost as soon as the user switches to a different model. The reasoning configuration appears to be overridden or reset on model change, rather than being preserved per model.

### Reproduction steps

1. Select a model.
2. Customize the reasoning settings for that model.
3. Switch to a different model.
4. Switch back to the original model.

Expected Behavior
The original model should retain its customized reasoning settings.

Actual Behavior
The reasoning settings are overridden or reset after changing models.

Impact
This makes it difficult to use multiple models with different reasoning configurations, requiring repeated manual adjustments.

Workaround
Create a separate profile for each model that requires a specific reasoning configuration.

### Provider

Kilo Code

### Model

Claude Sonnet 4.5

### System Information

_No response_

title: [JetBrains] IDE fails to detect changes made by Kilo (Background Editing) without manual "Reload from Disk"
body:
### Plugin Type

JetBrains Plugin

### App Version

4.136

### Description

When Kilo modifies a file while "Background Editing" is enabled, the JetBrains IDE does not automatically detect or reflect these changes in the editor. The editor view remains stale (showing the old content) even after Kilo confirms the task is complete.

The user is forced to manually execute "Reload All from Disk" (Ctrl+Alt+Y / Cmd+Option+Y) to see the changes applied by the agent.

### Reproduction steps

1. Open a project in a JetBrains IDE with the Kilo extension.
2. 
3. Ensure background editing (if applicable to the current mode/version) is active.
4. 
5. Instruct Kilo to modify an existing file (e.g., "Refactor the User class to add a login method").
6. 
7. Wait for Kilo to complete the edit.
8. 
9. Observation: The open editor window for that file still shows the code before the modification.
10. 
11. Action: Manually trigger File > Reload All from Disk.
12. 
13. Result: The IDE finally updates to show Kilo's changes.

Expected Behavior:
After Kilo writes to the file system, the extension should trigger a Virtual File System (VFS) refresh (e.g., VfsUtil.markDirtyAndRefresh or VirtualFile.refresh) so the IDE reflects the changes immediately without user intervention.

### Provider

all

### Model

all

### System Information

Extension Version: [4.136]

IDE: JetBrains (phpstorm)

OS: Ubuntu 24.04

title: No "reasoning effort" and "Tool call style" settings for Nano-GPT provider
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

4.137.0

### Description

When using Nano-GPT as the API provider in the Kilo Code VS Code extension, several important model capability settings are missing from the UI.

Specifically, options such as:

- Tool Call Protocol / Tool Call Style

- Model Reasoning Effort

- Related reasoning / tool-use configuration fields

do not appear at all when Nano-GPT is selected as the provider.

This behavior is consistent and reproducible and appears to affect multiple users.


🔧 Workaround

Currently, the only workaround is:

Configure Nano-GPT as an “OpenAI Compatible” provider

Manually specify the model name

In this setup:

Tool Call Protocol becomes available

Reasoning Effort can be configured

Models behave as expected


⚠️ Impact

This issue likely explains the major problems many users are experiencing with Nano-GPT models in Kilo Code, especially:

Broken or inconsistent tool calls

Poor reasoning behavior

Models silently ignoring intended capabilities

Without access to these settings, Nano-GPT is currently not usable for advanced workflows in Kilo.

### Reproduction steps

1. Open Kilo Code in VS Code

2. Open Provider / Model settings

3. Select Nano-GPT as the provider

4. Select any model that supports reasoning and/or tool calls

5. Inspect available configuration options

### Provider

Nano-GPT

### Model

all models

### System Information

🧾 Environment

Extension: Kilo Code (VS Code Extension)

Version: 4.137.0

Provider: Nano-GPT

OS: Windows 10 Pro 64bit
AMD Ryzen7-5800X
RTX 3060
64GB RAM

Output the plan as a markdown document saved to `docs/floating-obstacles-plan.md` **Constraints:** - Only analyze and plan; do NOT implement code - Use existing patterns from the codebase where possible - Ensure the design integrates naturally with the existing race system - Consider performance implications (this is a racing game) **Completion:** When finished, use the `attempt_completion` tool with a summary of the key architectural decisions and the location of the planning document. title: Cannot switch API provider back to Kilo Gateway
body:
### Plugin Type

VSCode Extension

### App Version

4.134.0 (1b365050)

### Description

After I installed the Kilo Code extension, I configured the API Provider to use VSCode LLM so I could try it with my Copilot subscription. After testing this out, I tried to switch the API provider back to Kilo Gateway. I clicked the "Log in at Kilo Code" button and authenticated using GitHub. When I switch back to VSCode, the Kilo Code Providers tab has reverted back to the VSCode LLM provider. If I reselect Kilo Gateway, it is unauthenticated.

### Reproduction steps

1. Select VSCode LLM as API provider.
2. Use it with this configuration.
3. Select Kilo Gateway as API provider.
4. Click "Log in with Kilo Code" button.
5. Go through auth flow in browser.
6. Switch back to VSCode.
7. Observe that VSCode LLM has been reselected as API provider.

### Provider

VSCode LLM

### Model

n/a

### System Information

OS: Windows 11
VSCode: 1.107.0
author: doxxx

comments.author: doxxx
comments.body:
I was able to copy the API key from my Kilo Code account on the website and paste that into the settings after switching to the Kilo Gateway provider.

I suspect that the issue might be that in the original reproduction steps I need to click Save in the Kilo Code settings after step 3. I haven't tested this though.
